Grounded is a multi-award-winning, B Corp certified brand activation agency — thriving at the intersection of brand experience, commercial innovation, sustainability and social impact.

We work with brands, retailers, startups and nonprofits all over the world — helping them transform purpose into profit, commercialize sustainability and drive behavior change at scale.

No hushing. No Washing. No tweaking around the edges.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Activating Purpose
Accelerating Impact
Get Grounded
Grounded is a multi-award-winning, B Corp certified brand activation agency — thriving at the intersection of brand experience, commercial innovation, sustainability and social impact.
We work with brands, retailers, startups and nonprofits all over the world — helping them transform purpose into profit, commercialize sustainability and drive behavior change at scale.
No hushing. No Washing. No tweaking around the edges.
Play Reel
Danielle Jezienicki
Vice President, Sustainability, Grove Collaborative

Case Studies – Get Inspired

Helping girls fight stereotypes - one photo at a time

Using a documentary to help protect the tribes of the Amazon

Kicking off a new decade of WhistlePig Whiskey tail first

Using one little fiber to help change the fashion industry

See all our current Case Studies
Our Services
We’re here to do good. And do it damn well. From global brands and retailers to gutsy startups and nonprofits, we find the intention-action gaps and help our clients to articulate purpose, activate their brands and accelerate impact. Purpose should pay and sustainability should be the unlock for creativity, strategic partnerships and commercial innovation – not a trade-off, a cost center or a compromise.
Our Services
Resources
Have a Question?
Ask Gaia. She’s our resident AI assistant.  She’s pretty sharp when it comes to advice and recommendations on how to get started or navigate the world of sustainability, social impact and corporate social responsibility.
Ask Gaia

Podcast

For lively, playful and provocative discussions around why it’s so hard to do the right things (and do the right things!) in business.

White Papers & Playbooks

Learn more about sustainable fashion, how to articulate brand purpose or activate sustainability with excellence at retail

How To Guides

Step by step guides to activating brand purpose, sustainability marketing, social impact and brand activism.

Articles & Blogs

An ever evolving library of thoughts, insights, provocations and points of view from the grounded team and guest authors.
It’s time to get grounded
Contact Us
Brand Purpose & Sustainability Agency | Grounded World
A brand purpose agency that helps brands activate purpose and accelerate impact with brand purpose, commercial strategy and social impact..
Grounded World | Brand Purpose & Social Impact Marketing Agency
Grounded World is a B-Corp Boutique Marketing Agency thriving at the intersection of brand purpose, commercial strategy, and social impact.
Grounded World | Brand Purpose & Social Impact Marketing Agency
Grounded World is a B-Corp Boutique Marketing Agency thriving at the intersection of brand purpose, commercial strategy, and social impact.

Get Grounded

For potential projects or engagements, feel free to schedule a call.

Discovery Call

For a specific request, submit a contact form.

A member of the team will get back to you within 24 hours. Sales and solution provider messages are automatically sent to spam.

Contact Form

For any other questions, media enquiries or if you’re interested in joining the team, please email us getgrounded@grounded.world

Make a Creative
Impact

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="100px||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="80px||||false|false" custom_padding_phone="8px||||false|false" locked="off" collapsed="off" global_colors_info="{}" custom_css_main_element_last_edited="on|phone"][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.18.0" _module_preset="default" text_font="|700|||||||" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="48px" header_2_font="|700|||||||" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font_size="48px" header_2_line_height="60px" width="100%" max_width="900px" custom_margin="||48px|0px|false|false" custom_margin_tablet="||48px|0px|false|false" custom_margin_phone="||0px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" text_font_size_tablet="48px" text_font_size_phone="" text_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" header_2_font_size_tablet="48px" header_2_font_size_phone="38px" header_2_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" header_2_line_height_tablet="60px" header_2_line_height_phone="48px" header_2_line_height_last_edited="on|phone" global_colors_info="{}"]

We are an award-winning creative impact agency

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/award-logo-1000px.png" title_text="award-logo-1000px" src_tablet="http://45.56.107.88:8080/stage.grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/award-logo-1000px.png" src_phone="http://45.56.107.88:8080/stage.grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/award-logo-500px-web.jpg" src_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="40px||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" text_line_height="30px" custom_margin="||0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="||0px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="||0px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|desktop" custom_padding="||0px|0px|false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px|0px|false|false" custom_padding_phone="||40px|0px|false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|tablet" global_colors_info="{}"]

What does a multi-award winning creative impact agency do, exactly? Well, we use the power of storytelling and ideas to change the world - because they're the only things that ever have.

And we like making them BIG, GOOD, FAST! Big ideas that change behavior and drive good growth - delivered at speed.

So, if you feel the urgency to act and you can imagine a better world, then join us. Because we’re hellbent on creating one.

The podcast for people who know that responsible business is messy, meaningful, and probably much harder than it should be!

In each episode, grounded co-founders, Heidi Schoeneck and Phil White sit down with founders, corporate change-makers, sustainability leaders and. social entrepreneurs who’ve been there and are doing it. They get stuck into honest and sometimes radically transparent conversations that corporations often try to avoid — about power, profit, fear and failure along with the many barriers and obstacles that often frustrate people and progress.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_code disabled_on="off|off|off" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="40px||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_code][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="40px||||false|false" hover_enabled="0" global_colors_info="{}" text_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" text_font_size_phone="15px" sticky_enabled="0"]

Sustainable Fashion

The fashion industry is at a turning point.

With new policies, regulations, and consumer expectations reshaping the industry, brands that adapt will thrive—those that don’t will fall behind.

Our free white paper, Mind the Gap: Policy to Profit in Fashion: How new rules can create commercial wins for fashion, is a guide for brand, innovation, marketing and sustainability professionals looking to: 

  • Navigate new ESG policies and sustainability regulations
  • Turn compliance into competitive advantage
  • Commercialize sustainability to deliver a triple bottom line impact

Developed with expert legal counsel and industry leaders and supported by Gaia, our real-time AI assistant that customizes recommendations to your brand or business, this white paper equips you with the data, insights, and practical guidance to navigate the most transformational policies, legislation and reporting standards and drive sustainable innovation.

This playbook includes  frameworks, tools, and inspirational case studies to help activate brand purpose and sustainability at retail with excellence.

The tools and frameworks inside are designed to enhance co-creation and collaboration between sustainability, brand, sales, trade-marketing and customer teams to close the intention-action gap at retail. 

The playbook is a culmination of three years worth of proprietary research, and consultation with some of the world’s largest brand owners and retailers.

This playbook includes  frameworks, tools, and inspirational case studies to help activate brand purpose and sustainability at retail with excellence.

The tools and frameworks inside are designed to enhance co-creation and collaboration between sustainability, brand, sales, trade-marketing and customer teams to close the intention-action gap at retail. 

The playbook is a culmination of three years worth of proprietary research, and consultation with some of the world’s largest brand owners and retailers.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Resources
Have a Question?
Ask Gaia. She’s our resident AI assistant.  She’s pretty sharp when it comes to advice and recommendations on how to get started or navigate the world of sustainability, social impact and corporate social responsibility.
Ask Gaia

Podcast

For lively, playful and provocative discussions around why it’s so hard to do the right things (and do the right things!) in business.

White Papers & Playbooks

Learn more about sustainable fashion, how to articulate brand purpose or activate sustainability with excellence at retail

How To Guides

Step by step guides to activating brand purpose, sustainability marketing, social impact and brand activism.

Articles & Blogs

An ever evolving library of thoughts, insights, provocations and points of view from the grounded team and guest authors.
Podcast
The podcast for people who know that responsible business is messy, meaningful, and probably much harder than it should be!
In each episode, grounded co-founders, Heidi Schoeneck and Phil White sit down with founders, corporate change-makers, sustainability leaders and. social entrepreneurs who’ve been there and are doing it. They get stuck into honest and sometimes radically transparent conversations that corporations often try to avoid — about power, profit, fear and failure along with the many barriers and obstacles that often frustrate people and progress.
APPLY TO BE A GUEST
White Papers & Playbooks
Sustainable Fashion
The fashion industry is at a turning point.
With new policies, regulations, and consumer expectations reshaping the industry, brands that adapt will thrive—those that don’t will fall behind.
Our free white paper, Mind the Gap: Policy to Profit in Fashion: How new rules can create commercial wins for fashion, is a guide for brand, innovation, marketing and sustainability professionals looking to: 

Navigate new ESG policies and sustainability regulations
Turn compliance into competitive advantage
Commercialize sustainability to deliver a triple bottom line impact

Developed with expert legal counsel and industry leaders and supported by Gaia, our real-time AI assistant that customizes recommendations to your brand or business, this white paper equips you with the data, insights, and practical guidance to navigate the most transformational policies, legislation and reporting standards and drive sustainable innovation.

Download
As Featured In….
Click on the logos below to read the articles 
Download Now
The BPP is a framework designed to help organizations and brands connect the WHY of purpose to the WAY of profit. Think of it as a business strategy / canvas on a page to connect purpose and sustainability with the commercial drivers of your business
The BPP is published by the ANA Center for Brand Purpose as a global best in class approach for articulating brand and corporate purpose.
Download Now
This playbook includes  frameworks, tools, and inspirational case studies to help activate brand purpose and sustainability at retail with excellence.
The tools and frameworks inside are designed to enhance co-creation and collaboration between sustainability, brand, sales, trade-marketing and customer teams to close the intention-action gap at retail. 
The playbook is a culmination of three years worth of proprietary research, and consultation with some of the world’s largest brand owners and retailers.
Download Now
The BPP is a framework designed to help organizations and brands connect the WHY of purpose to the WAY of profit. Think of it as a business strategy / canvas on a page to connect purpose and sustainability with the commercial drivers of your business
The BPP is published by the ANA Center for Brand Purpose as a global best in class approach for articulating brand and corporate purpose.
Download Now
This playbook includes  frameworks, tools, and inspirational case studies to help activate brand purpose and sustainability at retail with excellence.
The tools and frameworks inside are designed to enhance co-creation and collaboration between sustainability, brand, sales, trade-marketing and customer teams to close the intention-action gap at retail. 
The playbook is a culmination of three years worth of proprietary research, and consultation with some of the world’s largest brand owners and retailers.
How To Guides
Get grounded in the fundamentals of activating purpose, sustainability and social impact – whether you’re a brand, retailer, startup social enterprise or a nonprofit.

Activating Brand Purpose

Find out more about how to close the intention- action gap and change consumer behavior - especially at retail

Sustainability Marketing

Unpack what sustainability marketing is – along with some key challenges and inspirational case studies

Social Impact

Discover more about what a social impact agency does, and how to choose the right partner for your campaign

Brand Activism

Learn more about taking a stand and how to avoid the pitfalls
Articles & Blogs
Browse through the gallery of blogs and articles from guest authors and the grounded team or click on the tags to explore articles and points of view that match your area of interest.

Circular Cities in Action: On-the-Ground Learnings from Switzerland’s Urban Design Innovations

read more

Making Earth Day Every Day Shouldn’t Be This Hard | Kathleen Rogers

read more

Green Friday Brand Activation: Inspiring Sustainable Choices Instead of Black Friday

read more

A Future Without Cigarettes Shouldn’t Be This Hard | Jennifer Motles

read more

Circular Brand Activation in Switzerland: How QWSTION, ID Genève, and Zuriga Are Building Regenerative Brands

read more

Fair Price for Coffee Shouldn’t Be This Hard | Bob & Michelle Fish

read more

Page 1 of 712345...»Last »

Contact Us

PLEASE REVIEW THIS POLICY CAREFULLY

‍The following describes how GROUNDED WORLD LLC (“GROUNDED”) uses and disseminates information you provide through www.grounded.world.com and its sub-domains and affiliated sites, as well as Grounded World’s pages and accounts on Twitter®, Facebook®, LinkedIn®, Google®, Instagram®, Vimeo®, Medium® and YouTube® (the “Sites). If you ever have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at the information below. Please also review our full Terms and Conditions of Use, which also govern your use of the Sites. By using theses Sites, you are consenting to this Privacy Policy.

COLLECTION OF YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION.
‍
‍Unless we expressly note otherwise, we do not collect personally-identifiable information from users of these sites. When you visit these Sites, some information about your computer hardware and software is inherently automatically collected, such as your IP address, domain name, browser type, access time and referring website addresses. We typically do not use this information for any purpose, but an example of when we may use this information is in implementing improvements and analyzing the Sites and for troubleshooting purposes. We also utilize this information to monitor and improve services and to ensure that your use of the Sites is in compliance with our Terms of Use. Most of our services do not require any form of registration, allowing you to visit the Sites without telling us who you are. However, some services, such as email opt-ins may require you to provide us with Personal Data. In such a case, you may choose to without any Personal Data requested by us, but it may not be possible for you to gain access to certain parts of the site or content.  We require only the information that is reasonably required to enter into a contract with you. We will not require you to provide consent for any unnecessary processing as a condition of entering into a contract with us.

INTERNATIONAL PRIVACY LAWS AND YOUR RIGHTS UNDER THE GDPR

‍If you are visiting these Sites from outside the United States, please be aware that you are sending information to the United States where our servers are located. Information you submit may then be transferred within the United States or back out of the United States to other countries outside of your country of residence, depending on the type of information and how it is stored by us. These countries (including the United States) may not necessarily have data protection laws as comprehensive protective as your country of residence; however, our collection, storage, and use of your data will at all times continue to be governed by this Privacy Policy.
If you are a member of the European Union (EU), you have special rights under the GDPR. Those include: You have the right to object to the processing of your data and the right to portability of your data. All complaints must be sent to getgrounded@grounded.world. You also have the right to erasure, rectification, access, or to seek restrictions to the processing of your personal data in our system. To the extent you provide consent to our processing of your personal data, you have the right to withdraw that consent at any time. Any withdrawal of consent does not apply to data collected lawfully prior to such consent. You have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority containing jurisdiction over GDPR related issues.

COOKIES: WHAT THEY ARE, AND WHY THEY ARE NEEDED.

‍A cookie is a data text file sent from a website to your browser, for the purpose of identifying the user and allows access to portions of the website, thus alleviating the need to continually log in with your username and password. Cookies may be stored within your system. To the extent we use cookies, we can only access information from a cookie sent by one of the Sites, not other websites. We may use cookies to personalize your visit to our Site, because tracking usage allows us to best determine the needs of our clients, partners and affiliates.

OUR POLICY WITH MINORS.

Our website is not intended for individuals under the age of 18. We do not collect or maintain information from anyone known to be under the age of 18, and no part of this website is designed to attract anyone under the age of 18.  We do not sell products or services intended for purchase by children. If we discover or are otherwise notified that we have received any such information from a child in violation of this policy, we will delete that information.

DATA RETENTION

‍We only retain Personal Data collected from Users for as long as the User’s account is active or otherwise for a limited period of time as long as we need it to fulfill the purposes for which we have initially collected it, unless otherwise required by law. We will retain and use information as necessary to comply with our legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements for a period of 7 years.

USE OF YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION.

‍If you do choose to provide your personal information, we will not willingly share your information with companies outside our organization, except as described in this Privacy Policy.  You may at times receive communications from us related to products and services that we believe might interest you. While we believe these services may enhance your time spent at the Site, you will at all times have the option and ability to opt out from receiving these communications by specifically choosing to do so via a link which will be provided within emails that we send to you. We may disclose total aggregated user statistics in order to describe our services to potential advertisers, other third parties, affiliate companies, and for other lawful purposes.  The information we gather from you may be used in several ways, either now or in the future, to gain a better understanding of our Site’s users and their usage pattern as a whole, for site administration and troubleshooting, to process transactions, contest entries and other matters you initiate, to identify preferences in content and advertising, to target editorial, advertising or other content (such as promotions, special offers or other content) we think might be of interest to you. We may also use information we gather from you to communicate changes and improvements to our website or any registration you have made.

You have the right to request access to the information we have for you. You can do this by contacting us at getgrounded@grounded.world. We will make sure to provide you with a copy of the data we process about you. In order to comply with your request, we may ask you to verify your identity. We will fulfill your request by sending your copy electronically, unless the request specifies a different method. If you believe that the information we have about you is incorrect, or if you wish to remove your private information (such as an email address provided in an opt-in), you may contact us at getgrounded@grounded.world. Any data that is no longer needed for the purposes specified herein will be deleted.  We do not give away, sell, rent or lease any users’ personally identifiable information to any merchant, advertiser or web publisher. However, non-personally identifiable user information (such as usage pattern, browser type and your computer) may be shared with third party businesses or advertisers with which we have a business or contractual relationship. We reserve the right to disclose personal information when needed to comply with the law or a legal process, cooperate with investigations of purported unlawful activities, to identify persons violating the law, in connection with the sale of part or all of Grounded World’s or its affiliates assets, or to enforce our Terms of Use. Please keep in mind that if you disclose personally identifiable information in a public manner through the Site, this information may be collected and used by others accessing those portions of the Sites. We do not monitor information you disclose on the Site nor do we accept any liability associated with your voluntary disclosure of the same. You are responsible for reviewing the privacy statements and policies of other websites you choose to link to or from the Site, so that you may understand how those sites collect, use and store your information. We are not responsible for the privacy statements, policies or content of any other websites. Websites containing co-branding (referencing our name and a third party’s name) contain content delivered by the third party and not us.

NOTICE OF PRIVACY RIGHTS TO CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS.

‍California law requires that we provide you with a summary of your privacy rights under the California Online Privacy Protection Act (the “Act”) and the California Business and Professions Code. As required by the Act, we will provide you with the categories of personally identifiable information that we collect through this website and the categories of third party persons or entities with whom such personally identifiable information may be shared for direct marketing purposes at your request. California law requires us to inform you, at your request: (1) the categories of personally identifiable information we collect and what third parties we share that information with; (2) the names and addresses of those third parties; and (3) examples of the products marketed by those companies. The Act further requires us to allow you to control who we can and cannot share that information with. To obtain this information, please send a request by email or standard mail to the address found below. When contacting us please indicate your name, address, email address, and what personally identifiable information you do not want us to share with third parties. Please allow thirty (30) days for a response. Also, please note that there is no charge for controlling the sharing of your personally identifiable information or requesting this notice.

LIMITATIONS.

‍By using the Site you agree that we are not responsible for: (i) any disclosure of your personal information made by you to a third party through your use of the Site; (ii) any disclosure of your personal information obtained illegally from us; or (iii) any accidental disclosure of your personal information made by us.

POLICY CHANGES.

‍We may modify this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any modifications will be effective immediately when we post them. We will take steps to notify users of any modifications, however, you are responsible for reviewing any modified terms. When we update our Policy, we will note the date of revisions at the top of the Policy. Your continued use of a Site following any changes means you accept and agree to any changes. For your convenience and future reference, the date of the Privacy Policy is included so that you can compare any different versions of the Privacy Policy to determine any changes made to the Privacy Policy.

YOUR COMMENTS AND CONCERNS.

‍This website is operated by Grounded World LLC, 14 East 44th Street, 4th Floor, New York, NY 10017. All other feedback, comments, requests for technical support and other communications relating to the Sites should be directed to getgrounded@grounded.world.

If we discover or are otherwise notified that we have received any such information from a child in violation of this policy, we will delete that information.
DATA RETENTION
‍We only retain Personal Data collected from Users for as long as the User’s account is active or otherwise for a limited period of time as long as we need it to fulfill the purposes for which we have initially collected it, unless otherwise required by law. We will retain and use information as necessary to comply with our legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements for a period of 7 years.
USE OF YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION.
‍If you do choose to provide your personal information, we will not willingly share your information with companies outside our organization, except as described in this Privacy Policy.  You may at times receive communications from us related to products and services that we believe might interest you. While we believe these services may enhance your time spent at the Site, you will at all times have the option and ability to opt out from receiving these communications by specifically choosing to do so via a link which will be provided within emails that we send to you. We may disclose total aggregated user statistics in order to describe our services to potential advertisers, other third parties, affiliate companies, and for other lawful purposes.  The information we gather from you may be used in several ways, either now or in the future, to gain a better understanding of our Site’s users and their usage pattern as a whole, for site administration and troubleshooting, to process transactions, contest entries and other matters you initiate, to identify preferences in content and advertising, to target editorial, advertising or other content (such as promotions, special offers or other content) we think might be of interest to you. We may also use information we gather from you to communicate changes and improvements to our website or any registration you have made.
You have the right to request access to the information we have for you. You can do this by contacting us at getgrounded@grounded.world. We will make sure to provide you with a copy of the data we process about you. In order to comply with your request, we may ask you to verify your identity. We will fulfill your request by sending your copy electronically, unless the request specifies a different method. If you believe that the information we have about you is incorrect, or if you wish to remove your private information (such as an email address provided in an opt-in), you may contact us at getgrounded@grounded.world. Any data that is no longer needed for the purposes specified herein will be deleted.  We do not give away, sell, rent or lease any users’ personally identifiable information to any merchant, advertiser or web publisher. However, non-personally identifiable user information (such as usage pattern, browser type and your computer) may be shared with third party businesses or advertisers with which we have a business or contractual relationship. We reserve the right to disclose personal information when needed to comply with the law or a legal process, cooperate with investigations of purported unlawful activities, to identify persons violating the law, in connection with the sale of part or all of Grounded World’s or its affiliates assets, or to enforce our Terms of Use. Please keep in mind that if you disclose personally identifiable information in a public manner through the Site, this information may be collected and used by others accessing those portions of the Sites. We do not monitor information you disclose on the Site nor do we accept any liability associated with your voluntary disclosure of the same. You are responsible for reviewing the privacy statements and policies of other websites you choose to link to or from the Site, so that you may understand how those sites collect, use and store your information. We are not responsible for the privacy statements, policies or content of any other websites. Websites containing co-branding (referencing our name and a third party’s name) contain content delivered by the third party and not us.
NOTICE OF PRIVACY RIGHTS TO CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS.
‍California law requires that we provide you with a summary of your privacy rights under the California Online Privacy Protection Act (the “Act”) and the California Business and Professions Code. As required by the Act, we will provide you with the categories of personally identifiable information that we collect through this website and the categories of third party persons or entities with whom such personally identifiable information may be shared for direct marketing purposes at your request. California law requires us to inform you, at your request: (1) the categories of personally identifiable information we collect and what third parties we share that information with; (2) the names and addresses of those third parties; and (3) examples of the products marketed by those companies. The Act further requires us to allow you to control who we can and cannot share that information with. To obtain this information, please send a request by email or standard mail to the address found below. When contacting us please indicate your name, address, email address, and what personally identifiable information you do not want us to share with third parties. Please allow thirty (30) days for a response. Also, please note that there is no charge for controlling the sharing of your personally identifiable information or requesting this notice.
LIMITATIONS.
‍By using the Site you agree that we are not responsible for: (i) any disclosure of your personal information made by you to a third party through your use of the Site; (ii) any disclosure of your personal information obtained illegally from us; or (iii) any accidental disclosure of your personal information made by us.
POLICY CHANGES.
‍We may modify this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any modifications will be effective immediately when we post them. We will take steps to notify users of any modifications, however, you are responsible for reviewing any modified terms. When we update our Policy, we will note the date of revisions at the top of the Policy. Your continued use of a Site following any changes means you accept and agree to any changes. For your convenience and future reference, the date of the Privacy Policy is included so that you can compare any different versions of the Privacy Policy to determine any changes made to the Privacy Policy.
YOUR COMMENTS AND CONCERNS.
‍This website is operated by Grounded World LLC, 14 East 44th Street, 4th Floor, New York, NY 10017. All other feedback, comments, requests for technical support and other communications relating to the Sites should be directed to getgrounded@grounded.world.

It’s time to get grounded
Contact Us
It’s time to get grounded. privacy policy. Grounded World

PLEASE READ THESE TERMS CAREFULLY

‍These Terms of Use (the “Terms”) govern your use of and access to www.grounded.world and its sub-domains and affiliated sites, as well as Grounded World LLC’s (“Grounded”) pages and accounts on Facebook®, Twitter®, LinkedIn®, Google®, Vimeo®, Medium® and YouTube® (the “Sites). Please read both these Terms and my Privacy Policy carefully, which is incorporated into these Terms. By using any or all of the Sites, you accept and agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not want to agree to be bound by these Terms, do not use the Sites. We may modify these Terms from time to time, and any modifications will be effective immediately when we post them. All changes we make will be reflected in the date at the top of the document. You are responsible for reviewing any modified terms. Your continued use of a Site following any changes means you accept and agree to any changes. For your convenience and future reference, the date of the most recent revision of these Terms is listed above so that you may compare different versions to determine what, if any, changes have been made.

SITE CONTENT.

‍Grounded World LLC exclusively owns and controls the Sites, which provides information about my products and services and may, from time to time, provide access to educational materials pertaining to a variety of marketing, branding, design, communications and social innovations. You agree that, use or access to any or all of the Sites does not, standing alone, create any sort of representation or future promise. The unauthorized reproduction, use of, or theft of any content, written, photographic, or otherwise, is expressly prohibited. By using the Sites, you expressly agree to pay a fine of $50 per incident for any unauthorized use of our content, at the sole discretion of Grounded.

INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.

‍Unless explicitly stated otherwise, as between you and Grounded, Grounded owns all right, title, and interest in and to the Sites, including, without limitation, graphics, site content, design, organization, compilation and other matters related to or included on the Sites. Our name, Grounded World LLC and all related names, product and service names, logos, slogans and designs are our trademarks and you may not use these marks without our prior written permission. All other names, logos, product and service names, designs and slogans on the Sites are the trademarks of their respective owners and should not be used without those respective owners’ permission. You are granted a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the Sites and the resources available for download from the Website strictly in accordance with these Terms.

THIRD PARTY RIGHTS.

‍Content and materials posted to these Sites may be the copyrighted content of others (“Third Party Content”) that is used by Grounded either by permission or under Section 107 of the Copyright Act as “fair use” for purposes such as education and research. We respect the intellectual property of others and ask that you to do the same. Users must obtain permission from the owners of any Third Party content before copying, distributing or otherwise using those materials. Except as otherwise expressly permitted under copyright law, no copying, redistribution, retransmission, publication, or commercial exploitation of downloaded material will be permitted without the express permission of the copyright owner.
If you believe that your work has been copied on one or more of the Sites in a way that constitutes copyright infringement or otherwise violates your intellectual property rights, please contact us via email at the contact information listed below and provide the following: (i) identification of what is claimed to have been infringed; (ii) identification of what is claimed to be infringing; (iii) your contact information (or the contact information of the person we need to contact about the infringement); (iv) a statement that the person submitting the complaint is doing so with a good faith belief that use of the material in the manner complained of is not authorized by the owner, its agent, or the law; (v) a statement that the information provided is accurate, and under penalty of perjury; (vi) a physical or electronic signature of the person submitting the complaint; and (vii) if that person is not the owner of the content at issue, a statement that the person submitting the complaint is authorized to act on the owner’s behalf.

LINKING TO OUR SITES.

‍Anyone linking to the Sites must comply with all applicable laws and must not: (i) misrepresent its relationship with Grounded (ii) present false or misleading information about Grounded; or (iii) contain content that is reasonably considered profanity, offensive, defamatory, vulgar, or unlawful.

ADVERTISEMENTS AND LINKS.

‍We may at times include advertisements on the Sites. Your correspondence or business dealings with, or participation in promotions of, advertisers found on or through the Sites are solely between you and such advertiser. Any opinions, advice, statements, services, offers, or other information or content expressed or made available by advertisers, including information providers, or any other end users are those of the respective author(s) and not our own. You agree that we shall not be responsible or liable for any loss or damage of any sort incurred as a result of any such dealings or as the result of the presence of such advertisers. Any affiliate links that we link on the Sites will be clearly marked; however, we encourage you to reach out to us with any questions you may have regarding affiliate links.  You may find links to other websites on a Site. These links are provided solely as a convenience to you and not as an endorsement by Grounded of the contents on such third-party sites, and we expressly disclaim any representations regarding the content or accuracy of materials on such third-party websites. You acknowledge and agree that Grounded shall not be responsible or liable, directly or indirectly, for any damage or loss caused or alleged to be caused by or in connection with use of or reliance on any such content, goods or products available on or through any such linked site. You agree that it is your responsibility to evaluate the accuracy, completeness, or usefulness of any information, opinion, advice, etc., or other content available through such third-party sites.

DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATION OF LIABILITY.

‍THE SITES ARE PROVIDED ON AN “AS IS” AND “AS AVAILABLE” BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND. GROUNDED WORLD LLC, TOGETHER WITH ITS AFFILIATES, LICENSORS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, EMPLOYEES, AGENTS, OFFICERS OR DIRECTORS (THE “RELEASED PARTIES”), SPECIFICALLY DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT AND WARRANTIES THAT MAY ARISE OUT OF COURSE OF DEALING, COURSE OF PERFORMANCE, USAGE OR TRADE PRACTICE. THE RELEASED PARTIES DO NOT GUARANTEE THE RELIABILITY, ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, SAFETY, TIMELINESS, LEGALITY, USEFULNESS, ADEQUACY OR SUITABILITY OF ANY OF THE INFORMATION OR CONTENT ON THE SITES. ACCORDINGLY, YOU AGREE TO EXERCISE CAUTION, DISCRETION AND COMMON SENSE WHEN USING THE SITES. THE ENTIRE RISK FOR USE OF THE SITE AND/OR SERVICES IS BORNE BY YOU.TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL THE RELEASED PARTIES BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THE USE OF OR INABILITY TO ACCESS THE SITES, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF GOODWILL, WORK DISRUPTIONS, COMPUTER FAILURE OR MALFUNCTION, OR ANY AND ALL OTHER PERSONAL OR COMMERCIAL DAMAGES OR LOSSES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF, AND REGARDLESS OF THE LEGAL OR EQUITABLE THEORY (CONTRACT, TORT, BREACH OF WARRANTY OR OTHERWISE) UPON WHICH THE CLAIM IS BASED. THE RELEASED PARTIES ARE NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF THE POSTINGS OR ANY MATERIAL LINKED THROUGH THE SITES. YOUR SOLE REMEDY WITH RESPECT TO ANY CLAIM ARISING OUT OF YOUR USE OF THE SITES IS TO CEASE USING THE SITES.Some jurisdictions do not allow the disclaimer of implied warranties and/or limitations of liability, so a portion of this language may not apply to you. In such a case, any such disclaimer or limitation of liability is limited to the minimum extent permissible under applicable law.

CHOICE OF LAW AND VENUE.

‍These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of New York without regard to any conflict of laws principle. For any dispute regarding these Terms or the Sites, you agree to submit to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the federal and state courts located in New York, NY.

YOUR COMMENTS AND CONCERNS.

‍This website is operated by Grounded World LLC, 14 East 44th Street, 4th Floor, New York, NY, 10017 All other feedback, comments, requests for technical support and other communications relating to the Sites should be directed to: getgrounded@grounded.world

Thank you for visiting our Sites!

item.title: Fresh Air Fund: Because a Summer Can Last a Lifetime
item.dc:creator: phil_white
item.content:encoded:
[et_pb_section fb_built="1" inner_shadow="on" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="#000000" inner_module_alignment="center" custom_margin="0px||||false|false" custom_padding="40px||40px||false|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_color_top="#00aeef" box_shadow_horizontal_tablet="0px" box_shadow_vertical_tablet="0px" box_shadow_blur_tablet="40px" box_shadow_spread_tablet="0px" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.23.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" header_text_color="#FFFFFF" hover_enabled="0" global_colors_info="{}" sticky_enabled="0" header_line_height="1.1em"]

Fresh Air Fund: Because a Summer Can Last a Lifetime

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="17px" custom_margin="||30px||false|false" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"]

Refresh a heritage New York City nonprofit and create a campaign to help get underserved children out of NYC so they can improve relationships, open their minds and unlock their full potential.

Mary’s Center has been a leader in community health and economic growth in Washington DC and Maryland for over 30 years. As a healthcare center founded on caring for pregnant women— they now had a full service offering and needed to rebrand in order to reflect this expanded offering.

Part of a video series introducing #NEWwisdom — a movement and a multi-channel digital experience built to inspire and engage the NEW community through human stories. It consists of bite-sized films and social posts that share wisdom gathered from a person’s most crucible moments. The hub unveils the full, ever-growing collection of stories to keep the movement alive and thriving. Under the platform lives a clever bit of tech that feeds a dashboard, showing the learnings and interest of the community in order to inspire future stories and garner insight from the community.

item.title: Whistlepig Whiskey: The Boss Hog VII Magellan
item.dc:creator: phil_white
item.content:encoded:
[et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="||30px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.23.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" header_text_color="#FFFFFF" hover_enabled="0" global_colors_info="{}" header_line_height="1.1em" sticky_enabled="0"]

Whistlepig Whiskey: The Boss Hog VII - Magellan

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" text_text_color="#ffffff" text_font_size="17px" vertical_offset_tablet="0" horizontal_offset_tablet="0" custom_margin="||0px||false|false" z_index_tablet="0" text_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" text_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" text_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" link_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" link_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" link_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" ul_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" ul_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" ul_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" ol_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" ol_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" ol_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" quote_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" quote_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" quote_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_2_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_2_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_2_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_3_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_3_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_3_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_4_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_4_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_4_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_5_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_5_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_5_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_6_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_6_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_6_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" box_shadow_horizontal_tablet="0px" box_shadow_vertical_tablet="0px" box_shadow_blur_tablet="40px" box_shadow_spread_tablet="0px" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Boss Hog is Whistle Pig’s most premium, most unconventional whiskey and the Boss Hog VII would be the most stupendous yet. They needed a package as experiential as the whiskey itself. The premum design was inspired by Magellan’s quest circumnavigating the globe taking every person lucky enough to sample it on a world tour of the senses.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_video][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" admin_label="More work new section" item.title: Tree Free Greetings: Every Card Gives Back
item.dc:creator: phil_white
item.content:encoded:
[et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.23.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" header_text_color="#FFFFFF" hover_enabled="0" global_colors_info="{}" header_line_height="1.1em" sticky_enabled="0"]

Tree Free Greetings: Every Card Gives Back

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" text_text_color="#ffffff" text_font_size="17px" vertical_offset_tablet="0" horizontal_offset_tablet="0" custom_margin="||30px||false|false" z_index_tablet="0" text_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" text_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" text_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" link_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" link_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" link_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" ul_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" ul_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" ul_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" ol_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" ol_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" ol_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" quote_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" quote_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" quote_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_2_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_2_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_2_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_3_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_3_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_3_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_4_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_4_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_4_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_5_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_5_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_5_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_6_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_6_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_6_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" box_shadow_horizontal_tablet="0px" box_shadow_vertical_tablet="0px" box_shadow_blur_tablet="40px" box_shadow_spread_tablet="0px" global_colors_info="{}"]

Tree-Free Greetings is the only Greeting Card that gives back— not just to the person sending or receiving but also the planet and the causes people care about. Along with protecting the planet and fostering stronger human connections, we leveraged greeting cards to drive a campaign to raise $25M for nonprofits over the next 10 years by making it easier and more rewarding for people to write and send cards.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" Safe Ground is a Five-year campaign developed to bring attention and assistance to the survivors of armed conflict through the promotion of sport. The UNMAS division of the United Nation’s needed a way to garner investment and partners in order to turn minefields into soccer fields and bring communities back together through sport.

Delhaize: UnBEElieveables

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="17px" global_colors_info="{}"]

custom_padding="25px||25px|25px|true|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" header_font="|700|||||||" header_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_font_size="50px" header_line_height="1.2em" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font_size="24px" header_2_line_height="1.4em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="|20px|||false|false" header_font_size_tablet="37px" header_font_size_phone="25px" header_font_size_last_edited="on|desktop" header_line_height_tablet="1.2em" header_line_height_phone="1.2em" header_line_height_last_edited="on|phone" header_2_font_size_tablet="21px" header_2_font_size_phone="19px" header_2_font_size_last_edited="on|desktop" global_colors_info="{}"]

Brand Activism: How to Develop your Brand Activism Plan

Developing a brand activism plan is a strategic process that aligns your brand with social, environmental, or political causes, building deeper connections with your audience and contributing positively to society. Here's our step-by-step guide to help you navigate this journey:

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row use_custom_gutter="on" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|false|false" module_alignment_tablet="" module_alignment_phone="" module_alignment_last_edited="on|phone" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_button button_url="/contact-us/" button_text="Contact us" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" button_bg_color="gcid-secondary-color" button_font="|700||on|||||" custom_margin="|0px|0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="50px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="13px|20px|13px|20px|true|true" hover_enabled="0" locked="off" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-secondary-color%22:%91%22button_bg_color%22%93}" button_bg_enable_color__hover="on" custom_css_main_element_last_edited="on|phone" custom_css_main_element_phone="width: 100%!important;||text-align: center;" button_bg_color__hover_enabled="on|hover" sticky_enabled="0" button_bg_color__hover="#ff08cc" button_border_color__hover="#ff08cc"][/et_pb_button][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

Step 1. Understand Brand Activism

Defining Brand Activism

Brand activism consists of purposeful actions by companies to address fundamental concerns that reflect society's urgent problems, such as environmental or societal changes.

If your business is playing a significant role in driving social, political, economic, or environmental change or taking a ‘direct’ and ‘disruptive’ approach to influencing policy and legislation beyond advocacy, then it is engaging in brand activism. By taking a stand on a pressing issue (or issues), businesses can add a new facet to their brand mission and influence consumer preferences and purchasing decisions.

    [/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

    Historical Context

    One of the first companies to publicly declare that driving positive change in the world was as important as making a profit was The Body Shop.

    Founded in Brighton, England in 1976 by Anita Roddick, The Body Shop was a pioneer in cruelty-free and environmentally friendly cosmetics. Under Roddick's leadership, the company boldly aligned itself with significant social and environmental causes and body positivity.

    Their key campaigns and achievements include:

    • Partnering with Greenpeace in 1986 for the Save the Whale campaign to end whale hunting.
    • Launching a 1989 petition to halt the burning of Brazilian rainforests.
    • Advocating for the Ogoni People and campaigning against animal testing.
    • Promoting renewable energy and raising funds for victims of domestic violence.
    • Creating the Get Lippy campaign to support those living with HIV/AIDS.

    But perhaps its greatest success was a collaboration with ECPAT International to fight child trafficking, resulting in 24 countries adopting new legislation.

    The company’s unwavering commitment to human and animal rights, along with environmental sustainability, has built a fiercely loyal customer base over the years. Customers deeply value the alignment of their own beliefs with the brand’s mission, often willing to pay premium prices knowing their money supports meaningful causes.

    Ben & Jerry’s has also long funded and built relationships with civil rights leaders, giving the company a credible platform to use its voice and be heard. And like The Body Shop and Ben & Jerry's, activist brands such as Vega, Patagonia, and Tony’s Chocolonely have successfully embraced activism because social issues or environmental reform are the higher purpose at the heart of their brand.

    [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_line_height="1.9em" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font_size="31px" header_2_line_height="1.2em" background_color="#4dd9ff" custom_margin="-130px||||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding="70px|50px|70px|50px|true|true" custom_padding_tablet="50px|50px|50px|50px|true|true" custom_padding_phone="50px|30px|50px|30px|true|true" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" header_2_font_size_tablet="35px" header_2_font_size_phone="30px" header_2_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

    “Those who stand for nothing, fall for everything”

    Alexander Hamilton

    [/et_pb_text][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/vote-the.jpg" title_text="vote-the" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/stop-the-burning.jpg" title_text="stop-the-burning" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#000000" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" link_font="|600|||||||" link_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_line_height="1.2em" background_color="#4dd9ff" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="30px|40px|20px|40px|false|false" border_radii="on|20px|20px|20px|20px" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

    Contemporary Relevance

    In a world facing socially regressive policies driven out of political and religious division, brand activism offers companies seeking to be agents of change an avenue to directly engage in meaningful demonstration and solutions. Kantar's 2020 report found that 54% of consumers now expect brands to take an active role in social conversations about issues like #MeToo and racial injustices, with consumers also demanding meaningful action rather than empty solidarity.

    [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="#414854" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_direction="110deg" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height_tablet="" min_height_phone="" min_height_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="40px||40px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="20px||40px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="20px||40px||false|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" box_shadow_horizontal_tablet="0px" box_shadow_vertical_tablet="0px" box_shadow_blur_tablet="40px" box_shadow_spread_tablet="0px" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" background_size="initial" background_position="top_left" background_repeat="repeat" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_line_height="1.2em" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

    Step 2. Recognize the Business Benefits of Brand Activism

    “By choosing brands that align with their values, shoppers are voting with their wallets for the kinds of businesses they want in the world and paving the way toward a more sustainable and just economy. We’re in an unfortunate time where governments and institutions provide less and less moral leadership and we now expect to see it from the brands we buy.”

    From 'B the Change': A collaboration of B Lab, the B Corp community, and the movement of people using business as a force for good.

    Aligning with Consumer Values & Enhancing Brand Image

    Brands that have either been established with a higher purpose at their core, or those that have naturally evolved beyond corporate social responsibility to become values-driven companies, attract customers whose values align with their own and can more credibly and authentically take an ‘activist’ stance on issues that they and their consumers care about.

    Businesses that align their efforts with the same values as their audience not only build trust and equity but also gain long-term public support for their initiatives.

    This economic activism on the part of consumers elevates the purchase of everyday items and services from simple commercial transactions to an important expression of their moral code.

    [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/1595001865278.png" title_text="1595001865278" _builder_version="4.23.1" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_line_height="1.2em" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

    Driving Social Impact

    The depth of feeling that pushes people to the streets to protest a political issue or into the courtrooms in an attempt to achieve improvements in society should not be ignored or underestimated.

    Activism that addresses environmental change or societal challenges can act as a catalyst for public support and foster long-term loyalty from values-driven consumers.

    If the engine of commerce, fueled by social innovation and kept in check by an ever more conscious consumer, can create sustainable, scalable solutions for what the world needs and what people want, then brands can (and ostensibly should) become the most powerful instruments for change the world has ever seen!

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_line_height="1.3em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Step 3. Define Your Brand’s Purpose and Values

      Define your Purpose

      For companies seeking to address political issues or societal changes, understanding the intersection of their mission with urgent problems facing society is essential to crafting an authentic message.

      A good place to start is to draw a simple Venn diagram and then find the intersection between your mission (your brand purpose)  the money (biggest consumer barrier) and the magic (the issue or cultural challenge that you want to address.)

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/social-impact-graphic-1.jpg" title_text="social-impact-graphic-1" align="center" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" width="90%" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="50px||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_line_height="1.3em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Authenticity in Activism

      Corporate-driven initiatives that fail to align with a brand’s core mission and values risk appearing as marketing-driven stunts that impede improvements, damage brand equity, trust and reputation  instead of creating meaningful change.  

      In April 2023, Bud Light tried collaborating with transgender influencer Dylan Mulvaney on a social media promotional post. This sparked backlash from several prominent conservatives, leading many conservative figures and groups to call for a boycott of Bud Light. Bud Light lost  $1.4 billion.in U.S. beer sales and AB InBev lost over $27 billion in value following the controversy. They saw their U.S. market share slashed by half, largely fueled by U.S. retailers reducing shelf space for the brand.

      [/et_pb_text][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/bud-light.png" title_text="bud light" align="center" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/byd-sales.png" title_text="byd sales" align="center" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#000000" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_line_height="1.3em" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Activism isn't meant to be impulsive, reactionary or commercially destructive, but fear of change, misplaced sentiment  (or a clash of values) can sometimes drive individuals against brands and corporations that take a stand - if that position isn’t authentically connected to their purpose.

      [/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#000000" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" link_font="|600|||||||" link_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_line_height="1.2em" background_color="#4dd9ff" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="30px|40px|20px|40px|false|false" border_radii="on|20px|20px|20px|20px" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Engaging Stakeholders

      The Bud Light fiasco is a great example of failure to understand the brand’s key stakeholders (in this case, their core consumer base.)  Before taking a stand,  think about the entire ecosystem of stakeholders, how they are connected (or not!), and where the biggest intention/action gaps are.  Listen, learn, and seek to understand differing points of view and then (and only then)  see if it’s possible to complete that Venn diagram and find some common ground.

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" background_color="#414854" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_direction="110deg" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height="318px" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" background_size="initial" background_position="top_left" background_repeat="repeat" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_line_height="1.3em" background_layout="dark" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Step 4. Measure Your Impact and Adapt

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_layout="dark" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Monitoring and Evaluation

      Engaging in brand activism should always be driven by a moral compulsion rather than a commercial challenge. Taking a stance based on fear of failure, relevance or retribution alone does not pay off. There’s a big difference between an existential fear of standing by and not doing anything to change the status quo and then fear of doing something—anything—just in case you get found out.

      To ensure your efforts remain impactful, you can go back to that Venn diagram and measure the three most important dimensions of your activist stance and brand strategy.  How well do your key stakeholders understand and jive with your mission?  Have you managed to overcome the biggest intention-action gaps? and are you having a transformative impact on the lived experience of the core community that you serve?  Measure each (all can be done with a simple survey) and notice any shifts across each of these dimensions.

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_layout="dark" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Gathering Feedback

      Analyze the responses you get from your audience and stakeholders against these three dimensions and you'll find that you have both a strategy and a compass to help you navigate through the challenges and understand Whether your cause and commitment is transforming your purpose into profit.

      Continuous Improvement

      Be prepared to adapt your strategies based on your ‘flywheel of impact’ and make sure you stay up to speed with the changing political, economic, social, technological,  legal, and environmental (PESTLE) dynamics at play.

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_line_height="1.3em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Step 5. Understand What a Brand Activism Agency Can Do for Your Brand

      Partnering with a brand activism agency like grounded can help you understand the culture, context, and societal issues that your brand purpose can best help support and activate. Undertaking landscape assessments, stakeholder ecosystem mapping, intention/action gap analysis through to purpose articulation, campaign development, strategic partnerships, and measurement can all help you make sure you’re crystal clear on what you stand for, before taking a stand. 

      To find out more about our approach - simply roll over the tiles below.

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.18.0" _module_preset="default" width_tablet="" width_phone="89%" width_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding="|0px||0px|false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.18.0" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][simp_our_approach heading_1="Research" content_1="

      Assessing culture and the competitive landscape to find the intention-action gaps.

      • Culture, category, competitor and consumer landscape assessments
      • Intent-to-action gap research and analysis
      • Ecosystem mapping, need states, category growth drivers & go to market strategy
      " heading_2="Strategy" content_2="

      Connecting the ‘why’ of purpose to the ‘way’ of profit to tell your sustainability story and drive innovation.

      • Corporate, brand and social purpose articulation
      • Sustainability strategy, marketing, corporate narrative, storytelling and innovation sprints
      • Brand positioning architecture & archetyping
      " heading_3="Activation" content_3="

      Driving behavior change and intent at the moments that matter for colleagues, customers and consumers.

      • Brand identity, packaging, websites & design
      • Content, video, advertising, campaigns, events and experiences
      • Commerce (customer journeys, selling stories, brand and retail activation and tactical toolkits)
      " heading_4="Impact" content_4="

      Measuring impact, reporting on progress sparking engagement and facilitating collaboration and partnerships at scale

      • Sustainability & impact reporting
      • Commercial, non-profit and pre-competitive partnerships
      • Collaboration training, action labs, design sprints, speaking engagements and thought leadership
      " _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" module_alignment="center" global_colors_info="{}"]
