Home 9 Search query for: activism marketing agency
Brand Activism: How to Develop your Brand Activism Plan
Developing a brand activism plan is a strategic process that aligns your brand with social, environmental, or political causes, building deeper connections with your audience and contributing positively to society. Here’s our step-by-step guide to help you navigate this journey:
Contact us
Step 1. Understand Brand Activism

Defining Brand Activism
Brand activism consists of purposeful actions by companies to address fundamental concerns that reflect society’s urgent problems, such as environmental or societal changes.
If your business is playing a significant role in driving social, political, economic, or environmental change or taking a ‘direct’ and ‘disruptive’ approach to influencing policy and legislation beyond advocacy, then it is engaging in brand activism. By taking a stand on a pressing issue (or issues), businesses can add a new facet to their brand mission and influence consumer preferences and purchasing decisions.

Historical Context
One of the first companies to publicly declare that driving positive change in the world was as important as making a profit was The Body Shop.
Founded in Brighton, England in 1976 by Anita Roddick, The Body Shop was a pioneer in cruelty-free and environmentally friendly cosmetics. Under Roddick’s leadership, the company boldly aligned itself with significant social and environmental causes and body positivity.
Their key campaigns and achievements include:

Partnering with Greenpeace in 1986 for the Save the Whale campaign to end whale hunting.
Launching a 1989 petition to halt the burning of Brazilian rainforests.
Advocating for the Ogoni People and campaigning against animal testing.
Promoting renewable energy and raising funds for victims of domestic violence.
Creating the Get Lippy campaign to support those living with HIV/AIDS.

But perhaps its greatest success was a collaboration with ECPAT International to fight child trafficking, resulting in 24 countries adopting new legislation.
The company’s unwavering commitment to human and animal rights, along with environmental sustainability, has built a fiercely loyal customer base over the years. Customers deeply value the alignment of their own beliefs with the brand’s mission, often willing to pay premium prices knowing their money supports meaningful causes.
Ben & Jerry’s has also long funded and built relationships with civil rights leaders, giving the company a credible platform to use its voice and be heard. And like The Body Shop and Ben & Jerry’s, activist brands such as Vega, Patagonia, and Tony’s Chocolonely have successfully embraced activism because social issues or environmental reform are the higher purpose at the heart of their brand.

“Those who stand for nothing, fall for everything”
Alexander Hamilton
Contemporary Relevance
In a world facing socially regressive policies driven out of political and religious division, brand activism offers companies seeking to be agents of change an avenue to directly engage in meaningful demonstration and solutions. Kantar’s 2020 report found that 54% of consumers now expect brands to take an active role in social conversations about issues like #MeToo and racial injustices, with consumers also demanding meaningful action rather than empty solidarity.
Step 2. Recognize the Business Benefits of Brand Activism
“By choosing brands that align with their values, shoppers are voting with their wallets for the kinds of businesses they want in the world and paving the way toward a more sustainable and just economy. We’re in an unfortunate time where governments and institutions provide less and less moral leadership and we now expect to see it from the brands we buy.”
From ‘B the Change’: A collaboration of B Lab, the B Corp community, and the movement of people using business as a force for good.
Aligning with Consumer Values & Enhancing Brand Image
Brands that have either been established with a higher purpose at their core, or those that have naturally evolved beyond corporate social responsibility to become values-driven companies, attract customers whose values align with their own and can more credibly and authentically take an ‘activist’ stance on issues that they and their consumers care about.
Businesses that align their efforts with the same values as their audience not only build trust and equity but also gain long-term public support for their initiatives.
This economic activism on the part of consumers elevates the purchase of everyday items and services from simple commercial transactions to an important expression of their moral code.
Driving Social Impact
The depth of feeling that pushes people to the streets to protest a political issue or into the courtrooms in an attempt to achieve improvements in society should not be ignored or underestimated.
Activism that addresses environmental change or societal challenges can act as a catalyst for public support and foster long-term loyalty from values-driven consumers.
If the engine of commerce, fueled by social innovation and kept in check by an ever more conscious consumer, can create sustainable, scalable solutions for what the world needs and what people want, then brands can (and ostensibly should) become the most powerful instruments for change the world has ever seen!
Step 3. Define Your Brand’s Purpose and Values
Define your Purpose
For companies seeking to address political issues or societal changes, understanding the intersection of their mission with urgent problems facing society is essential to crafting an authentic message.
A good place to start is to draw a simple Venn diagram and then find the intersection between your mission (your brand purpose)  the money (biggest consumer barrier) and the magic (the issue or cultural challenge that you want to address.)
Authenticity in Activism
Corporate-driven initiatives that fail to align with a brand’s core mission and values risk appearing as marketing-driven stunts that impede improvements, damage brand equity, trust and reputation  instead of creating meaningful change.  
In April 2023, Bud Light tried collaborating with transgender influencer Dylan Mulvaney on a social media promotional post. This sparked backlash from several prominent conservatives, leading many conservative figures and groups to call for a boycott of Bud Light. Bud Light lost  $1.4 billion.in U.S. beer sales and AB InBev lost over $27 billion in value following the controversy. They saw their U.S. market share slashed by half, largely fueled by U.S. retailers reducing shelf space for the brand.
Activism isn’t meant to be impulsive, reactionary or commercially destructive, but fear of change, misplaced sentiment  (or a clash of values) can sometimes drive individuals against brands and corporations that take a stand – if that position isn’t authentically connected to their purpose.
Engaging Stakeholders
The Bud Light fiasco is a great example of failure to understand the brand’s key stakeholders (in this case, their core consumer base.)  Before taking a stand,  think about the entire ecosystem of stakeholders, how they are connected (or not!), and where the biggest intention/action gaps are.  Listen, learn, and seek to understand differing points of view and then (and only then)  see if it’s possible to complete that Venn diagram and find some common ground.
Step 4. Measure Your Impact and Adapt
Monitoring and Evaluation
Engaging in brand activism should always be driven by a moral compulsion rather than a commercial challenge. Taking a stance based on fear of failure, relevance or retribution alone does not pay off. There’s a big difference between an existential fear of standing by and not doing anything to change the status quo and then fear of doing something—anything—just in case you get found out.
To ensure your efforts remain impactful, you can go back to that Venn diagram and measure the three most important dimensions of your activist stance and brand strategy.  How well do your key stakeholders understand and jive with your mission?  Have you managed to overcome the biggest intention-action gaps? and are you having a transformative impact on the lived experience of the core community that you serve?  Measure each (all can be done with a simple survey) and notice any shifts across each of these dimensions.
Gathering Feedback
Analyze the responses you get from your audience and stakeholders against these three dimensions and you’ll find that you have both a strategy and a compass to help you navigate through the challenges and understand Whether your cause and commitment is transforming your purpose into profit.
Continuous Improvement
Be prepared to adapt your strategies based on your ‘flywheel of impact’ and make sure you stay up to speed with the changing political, economic, social, technological,  legal, and environmental (PESTLE) dynamics at play.
Step 5. Understand What a Brand Activism Agency Can Do for Your Brand
Partnering with a brand activism agency like grounded can help you understand the culture, context, and societal issues that your brand purpose can best help support and activate. Undertaking landscape assessments, stakeholder ecosystem mapping, intention/action gap analysis through to purpose articulation, campaign development, strategic partnerships, and measurement can all help you make sure you’re crystal clear on what you stand for, before taking a stand. 
To find out more about our approach – simply roll over the tiles below.

Research

Assessing culture and the competitive landscape to find the intention-action gaps.

Culture, category, competitor and consumer landscape assessments
Intent-to-action gap research and analysis
Ecosystem mapping, need states, category growth drivers & go to market strategy

Find Out More

Strategy

Connecting the ‘why’ of purpose to the ‘way’ of profit to tell your sustainability story and drive innovation.

Corporate, brand and social purpose articulation
Sustainability strategy, marketing, corporate narrative, storytelling and innovation sprints
Brand positioning architecture & archetyping

Find Out More

Activation

Driving behavior change and intent at the moments that matter for colleagues, customers and consumers.

Brand identity, packaging, websites & design
Content, video, advertising, campaigns, events and experiences
Commerce (customer journeys, selling stories, brand and retail activation and tactical toolkits)

Find Out More

Impact

Measuring impact, reporting on progress sparking engagement and facilitating collaboration and partnerships at scale

Sustainability & impact reporting

Collaboration training, action labs, design sprints, speaking engagements and thought leadership

 A Complete Guide to Startup Branding

How do you go about branding your business, if it's a startup? With a start-up marketing agency.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_3,1_3,1_3" use_custom_gutter="on" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_button button_url="/contact-us/" button_text="Contact us" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" button_bg_color="gcid-secondary-color" button_font="|700||on|||||" custom_margin="|0px|0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="50px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="13px|20px|13px|20px|true|true" locked="off" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-secondary-color%22:%91%22button_bg_color%22%93}" button_bg_enable_color__hover="on" custom_css_main_element_last_edited="on|phone" custom_css_main_element_phone="width: 100%!important;||text-align: center;" button_bg_color__hover_enabled="on|hover" button_bg_color__hover="#ff08cc" button_border_color__hover="#ff08cc"][/et_pb_button][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_button button_url="https://grounded.world/brand-marketing-agency-services/" button_text="Build Your Roadmap" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" button_bg_color="#000000" button_font="|700||on|||||" custom_margin="0px|0px|0px|0px|false|false" custom_padding="13px|16px|13px|16px|true|true" global_colors_info="{}" button_bg_enable_color__hover="on" button_border_color__hover="#ff08cc"][/et_pb_button][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.0" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#000000" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_font="|700|||||||" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" global_colors_info="{}"]A startup or social enterprise with a ‘massive transformative purpose’ stands the best chance of being successful. When branding for start-ups you have to communicate that purpose effectively. If you don’t, its influence will be small and its impact will be difficult to scale. This is why branding for startups is so important and why you need a startup marketing agency that has been there and done it – many times.  Turning your purpose into a compelling commercial value proposition is difficult. Getting investors, donors and customers excited about your products and services is even more so. Connecting both together and then packaging it all up ready to go to market is the ultimate test — and one of the most important aspects of social entrepreneurship. Find an agency that knows how to connect the ‘why’ of your purpose to the ‘way’ of profit, appeal to investors and raise team engagement and commitment. A good startup marketing agency also knows how to build in rigor and resilience into your brand articulation. No 5-year-plan will succeed and no addressable market will matter if you can’t instantly and clearly articulate your brand purpose and core value proposition. A clear, disruptive and transformative purpose and a clear and compelling value proposition allows you to shift your brand from a nice idea or a ‘point of view’ about the world to a business driver of social and economic impact. Your startup or social enterprise then has the DNA to prevail and become a force for good so you can go on and make some of the world’s biggest challenges into the world’s biggest business opportunities. That’s what a good startup marketing agency can help you do.  

The Belief, Purpose & Pursuits Framework for Startup Branding

The BPP framework (Belief, Purpose & Pursuits) unites the ‘why’ of purpose with the ‘way’ of profit. From the top down and bottom up, it synchronizes people, product, process and performance behind purpose, providing the basis for innovation and the narrative for internal and external engagement. It’s goal is to give brands and organizations the focus, inspiration and flexibility to think, behave and govern more responsibly — more like a social enterprise.
[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_line_height="1.9em" header_2_font="|700|||||||" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font_size="38px" header_2_line_height="1.2em" background_color="#4dd9ff" custom_margin="-130px||||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding="90px|60px|90px|60px|true|true" custom_padding_tablet="50px|50px|50px|50px|true|true" custom_padding_phone="50px|30px|50px|30px|true|true" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" header_2_font_size_tablet="29px" header_2_font_size_phone="26px" header_2_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

If your branding doesn’t stack up then neither will your business.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="#414854" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_direction="110deg" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height="318px" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" background_size="initial" background_position="top_left" background_repeat="repeat" custom_margin="0px||20px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.17.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

How it works:

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.16" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

THE BELIEF:

This is what you believe about the world. It is the single-minded profoundly human belief that anchors your ambition and provides the inspiration for your purpose.

It should be human.
It should be truthful.
It should be universal.

And it should set-up a tension by addressing the problem or barrier for your purpose to resolve.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.16" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

THE PURPOSE:

This is should answer the question – why do we exist? Purpose should flow through every level of the business. It should express how you will affect culture, serve people or change the world for the better in some way.

It should be simple.
It should be focussed.
It should be transformational.

It should make you feel that your job is never done and if it does, then it should make you redundant.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.16" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_margin="21px|||||" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

THE PURSUITS:

These are three key things that your brand or organization should be actively pursuing day in and day out. They should drive decision-making, inform business strategy, focus resources and in turn, help shape your values and behaviors.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_3,1_3,1_3" make_equal="on" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.17.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Product:

This is what your resources and talents should be focused on: producing, selling, and developing.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.18.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Platform:

This is the main communications and engagement vehicle that you use to go to market. It should empower the end user, customer, client or donor to control the kind of experience they want.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.17.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Impact:

The difference that your brand or organization can make in the world if your product and platform can generate a sustainable revenue.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#000000" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_4_font="||on||||||" header_4_font_size="17px" header_4_line_height="1.3em" global_colors_info="{}"]

Here’s an example:

OceanCycle is a startup, B-corp certified, social enterprise. Here’s how our BPP framework has helped their startup branding. 

 

BELIEF:
We will only put an end to ocean plastic by 2030 if we stop the flow of new plastic entering the ocean along with the marketing spin and misinformation around what can actually be retrieved and recycled.

PURPOSE:
‘Clean Up’ Ocean Plastic for Good.

PURSUITS:
Consultation:
We create marketplace demand for ocean bound plastic and work with clients to help them build the recycling infrastructures, ecosystems and supply chains necessary for driving scale and circularity.

Certification:
We certify ocean bound plastic material from collection through to manufacture — setting the standard, guaranteeing its authenticity and traceability across the entire chain of custody.

Community:
We help our clients realize the commercial value of ocean bound plastic through direct engagement with the communities where plastic is collected — resulting in jobs and income that improve livelihoods and lift families out of poverty.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/OC-logo.png" alt="ocean cycle company logo" title_text="OC-logo" align="center" _builder_version="4.23.1" _module_preset="default" width="85%" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/water-fork.jpg" alt="startup branding purpose - grounded world agency" title_text="startup branding purpose - grounded world agency" align="center" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" width="85%" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="#414854" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_direction="110deg" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height="318px" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_width_bottom="40px" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" background_size="initial" background_position="top_left" background_repeat="repeat" custom_margin="0px||20px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.18.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

How Grounded Can Help You Develop Your Startup Marketing Plan

The BPP framework gives start-ups, brands, nonprofits and social enterprises the focus, inspiration and flexibility they need to turn branding into action. These actions can scale social innovations and campaigns into instruments that make the world a better place to be.

It is the first and most important step in the articulation of your core value proposition. And if done well, it can drive decision making from the inside-out. It connects what you believe about the world to your purpose. It transforms that purpose into profit through the pursuits and creates value by doing good. 

Get in touch if you would like help developing your own BPP. It starts with discovery, a few stakeholder interviews, a workshop and some iteration and can usually be completed in 3-4 weeks.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
A Complete Guide to Startup Branding
How do you go about branding your business, if it’s a startup? With a start-up marketing agency.
Contact us
Build Your Roadmap
A startup or social enterprise with a ‘massive transformative purpose’ stands the best chance of being successful. When branding for start-ups you have to communicate that purpose effectively. If you don’t, its influence will be small and its impact will be difficult to scale. This is why branding for startups is so important and why you need a startup marketing agency that has been there and done it – many times. 

Turning your purpose into a compelling commercial value proposition is difficult. Getting investors, donors and customers excited about your products and services is even more so. Connecting both together and then packaging it all up ready to go to market is the ultimate test — and one of the most important aspects of social entrepreneurship.

Find an agency that knows how to connect the ‘why’ of your purpose to the ‘way’ of profit, appeal to investors and raise team engagement and commitment. A good startup marketing agency also knows how to build in rigor and resilience into your brand articulation. No 5-year-plan will succeed and no addressable market will matter if you can’t instantly and clearly articulate your brand purpose and core value proposition.

A clear, disruptive and transformative purpose and a clear and compelling value proposition allows you to shift your brand from a nice idea or a ‘point of view’ about the world to a business driver of social and economic impact.

Your startup or social enterprise then has the DNA to prevail and become a force for good so you can go on and make some of the world’s biggest challenges into the world’s biggest business opportunities. That’s what a good startup marketing agency can help you do.

 
The Belief, Purpose & Pursuits Framework for Startup Branding
The BPP framework (Belief, Purpose & Pursuits) unites the ‘why’ of purpose with the ‘way’ of profit. From the top down and bottom up, it synchronizes people, product, process and performance behind purpose, providing the basis for innovation and the narrative for internal and external engagement. It’s goal is to give brands and organizations the focus, inspiration and flexibility to think, behave and govern more responsibly — more like a social enterprise.

If your branding doesn’t stack up then neither will your business.
How it works:
THE BELIEF:
This is what you believe about the world. It is the single-minded profoundly human belief that anchors your ambition and provides the inspiration for your purpose.
It should be human.It should be truthful.It should be universal.
And it should set-up a tension by addressing the problem or barrier for your purpose to resolve.
THE PURPOSE:
This is should answer the question – why do we exist? Purpose should flow through every level of the business. It should express how you will affect culture, serve people or change the world for the better in some way.
It should be simple.It should be focussed.It should be transformational.
It should make you feel that your job is never done and if it does, then it should make you redundant.
THE PURSUITS:
These are three key things that your brand or organization should be actively pursuing day in and day out. They should drive decision-making, inform business strategy, focus resources and in turn, help shape your values and behaviors.
The Product:
This is what your resources and talents should be focused on: producing, selling, and developing.
The Platform:
This is the main communications and engagement vehicle that you use to go to market. It should empower the end user, customer, client or donor to control the kind of experience they want.
The Impact:
The difference that your brand or organization can make in the world if your product and platform can generate a sustainable revenue.
Here’s an example:
OceanCycle is a startup, B-corp certified, social enterprise. Here’s how our BPP framework has helped their startup branding. 
 
BELIEF:We will only put an end to ocean plastic by 2030 if we stop the flow of new plastic entering the ocean along with the marketing spin and misinformation around what can actually be retrieved and recycled.
PURPOSE:‘Clean Up’ Ocean Plastic for Good.
PURSUITS:Consultation:We create marketplace demand for ocean bound plastic and work with clients to help them build the recycling infrastructures, ecosystems and supply chains necessary for driving scale and circularity.
Certification:We certify ocean bound plastic material from collection through to manufacture — setting the standard, guaranteeing its authenticity and traceability across the entire chain of custody.
Community:We help our clients realize the commercial value of ocean bound plastic through direct engagement with the communities where plastic is collected — resulting in jobs and income that improve livelihoods and lift families out of poverty.
How Grounded Can Help You Develop Your Startup Marketing Plan
The BPP framework gives start-ups, brands, nonprofits and social enterprises the focus, inspiration and flexibility they need to turn branding into action. These actions can scale social innovations and campaigns into instruments that make the world a better place to be.
It is the first and most important step in the articulation of your core value proposition. And if done well, it can drive decision making from the inside-out. It connects what you believe about the world to your purpose. It transforms that purpose into profit through the pursuits and creates value by doing good. 
Get in touch if you would like help developing your own BPP. It starts with discovery, a few stakeholder interviews, a workshop and some iteration and can usually be completed in 3-4 weeks.
Useful links 
> Download your BPP Guide HERE
> Learn more about OceanCycle HERE
> Become a B-corp HERE
It’s time to get grounded
Contact Us

item.title: Plan USA: Girls vs The Machine
item.dc:creator: phil_white
item.content:encoded:

Plan USA: Girls vs The Machine

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" text_text_color="#ffffff" text_font_size="17px" vertical_offset_tablet="0" horizontal_offset_tablet="0" z_index_tablet="0" text_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" text_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" text_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" link_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" link_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" link_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" ul_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" ul_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" ul_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" ol_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" ol_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" ol_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" quote_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" quote_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" quote_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_2_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_2_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_2_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_3_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_3_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_3_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_4_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_4_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_4_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_5_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_5_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_5_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" header_6_text_shadow_horizontal_length_tablet="0px" header_6_text_shadow_vertical_length_tablet="0px" header_6_text_shadow_blur_strength_tablet="1px" box_shadow_horizontal_tablet="0px" box_shadow_vertical_tablet="0px" box_shadow_blur_tablet="40px" box_shadow_spread_tablet="0px" global_colors_info="{}"]

We partnered PLAN USA with Unsplash, the internet’s source of freely usable visuals, to reframe the way girls are represented in media all around the world. Because if we can change the way the world sees girls, we will change the way girls see themselves.

Plan USA x Unsplash Social Impact Case Study | Grounded World
Discover how Grounded, a social impact agency, partnered Plan International USA with Unsplash to reshape the media representation of girls.

global_colors_info="{}"]

A Complete Guide to Nonprofit Branding & Marketing

Learn more about nonprofit branding, marketing and the benefits of working with an agency for both. 

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_3,1_3,1_3" use_custom_gutter="on" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|false|false" module_alignment_tablet="" module_alignment_phone="" module_alignment_last_edited="on|phone" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_3" button_bg_color="gcid-secondary-color" button_font="|700||on|||||" custom_margin="|0px|0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="50px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="13px|20px|13px|20px|true|true" hover_enabled="0" locked="off" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-secondary-color%22:%91%22button_bg_color%22%93}" button_bg_enable_color__hover="on" button_bg_color__hover="#ff08cc" button_border_color__hover="#ff08cc"] [/et_pb_button][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.25.2" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_font="|700|||||||" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" global_colors_info="{}"]

Think about the WWF panda, Amnesty International’s flickering candle surrounded by barbed wire or the Peace Corps dove logo, which fuses the powerful imagery of the national flag and a globally recognised symbol of peace. Most would consider these good examples of the power of branding in the nonprofit sector.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/3-logos.jpg" title_text="3-logos" _builder_version="4.23.1" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_font="|700|||||||" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" global_colors_info="{}"]

Yet truly powerful branding in the deeper more cultural sense,  goes beyond a nonprofit’s visual identity. It’s more to do with the mission statement behind it all that underpins its role, meaning, difference and connection to the world and the people it serves. 

It’s that – as much as the visual identity – that people hold in their heads and why ultimately,  they put their hands in their pockets.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15px" text_line_height="1.9em" header_2_font="|700|on||||||" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font_size="38px" header_2_line_height="1.2em" background_color="#4dd9ff" custom_margin="-130px||||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding="90px|60px|90px|60px|true|true" custom_padding_tablet="50px|50px|50px|50px|true|true" custom_padding_phone="50px|30px|50px|30px|true|true" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" header_2_font_size_tablet="29px" header_2_font_size_phone="26px" header_2_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

What is the role of branding and marketing for nonprofits and social enterprises?

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="#414854" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_direction="110deg" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height="318px" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.18.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

How can a nonprofit marketing agencies and their branding efforts help to achieve wider goals? 

In an age where brand activism and cancel culture are growing,  along with the demand for products and services from companies whose values align more with our own, it is hardly surprising that branding and marketing for nonprofits and social enterprises is growing in complexity too. 

Previously,  branding and marketing for nonprofits often meant throwing your logo on a few pens and hoping that their repeated visibility and use for household shopping lists would mean that your organisation came to mind when people were feeling altruistic.  

In other words, branding was more of an executional tool for fundraising than anything else – rather than the reason the organization should exist or be fundraising in the first place. And today that’s the predicament many nonprofits still find themselves in. 

Good branding and marketing is often subservient to fundraising when in fact,  as any nonprofit marketing agency  will tell you,  to build equity and unlock revenue – the opposite should be the case. 

Today, an increasing number of nonprofits are exploring “the wider, strategic roles that brands can play: driving broad, long-term social goals, while strengthening internal identity, cohesion, and capacity.” It’s no longer about simply achieving fundraising targets through branding projects. 

Both established nonprofits and newly emerging ones are shifting the focus of their branding projects away from simply achieving fundraising targets and towards the achievement of social impact goals and greater equity, cultural and organisational cohesion. In essence, they are realising that having an MTP (massive transformative purpose) and uniting everyone in their organization to work towards this purpose,  is a far more valuable role for branding to play.

That’s good news for the industry, but even better news for people and the planet. 

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/massive.jpg" title_text="massive" _builder_version="4.18.0" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_4_font="||on||||||" header_4_font_size="17px" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_layout="dark" global_colors_info="{}"]

How do you create a strong brand identity for a nonprofit or social enterprise?

In contrast to most for-profit brands, nonprofit organizations start with a desire to further their mission and make a sustainable impact, not make money. There are no shareholders, only stakeholders whose lives are often tied directly to the organization’s ability to serve them and raise money.

Think of World Food Programme, Unicef or St Jude’s Hospital or the Red Cross. Whether they are raising funds and awareness to feed children in wa-torn countries or funding research into cancer, they are established by people with a desire to make a positive difference in the world. But there are a lot of these good causes and a lot of organisations trying to advance very similar missions, 1.5m in the US alone. 

Creating a strong brand identity will enable you to differentiate yourself from your competition and talk to your target audience effectively to cultivate ongoing loyalty and trust. 

A nonprofit with a strong brand has better visibility, is more mentally accessible,  has a stronger reputation and so access to more resources and a greater influence on how those resources are deployed.  

Get the branding right and you will get more donations and have your pick of the smartest, most dedicated employees and volunteers.

Tips for Building a Strong Nonprofit Brand

1. Nail your brand purpose and mission statement (and yes, they are different)

Your purpose is why you exist and your mission statement is what you promise to do.  Both should be the heart of your nonprofit brand and you should refer back to them whenever you need to make important strategic decisions. Request input from donors, board members, stakeholders, employees and volunteers when crafting your brand purpose and mission statement, as this will facilitate valuable organizational cohesion.

2. Know your Audience

Successful nonprofit branding comes from knowing your target audience. Take the time to understand, not only your current or potential donors, but also the people who may be prepared to give up their time to volunteer for your organization or spread the word about what you do. Tap into their beliefs, their mindsets and and their motivations for doing whatever they do and you will often find a universal belief or mindset that unites them all and provides a powerful platform for your purpose and future brand building.

3. Create a Nonprofit Branding Guide

When you have worked hard to establish exactly what your brand stands for and carefully designed and selected a logo, fonts, colours imagery and photography that you feel perfectly reflect your organization, the last thing you want is for someone to inadvertently send the wrong branding elements to press or use the wrong tone in an email campaign. Make it easy for employees and volunteers to get it right by creating a comprehensive branding guide, which lets them know what files to use where and provides clear guidance about the style and tone of each different content type your organization uses.

4. Tell Your Stories 

Whilst your stats and the clarity of your communication will convince your audience that you are working hard and using their donated dollars to good effect, you also need to communicate the day-to-day challenges your nonprofit and its beneficiaries face and the best way to do that is through storytelling. 

These can be in social posts, videos, blog posts or printed material, just be honest (no one wants an exaggerated sales pitch) and remember that focusing on a main protagonist will always generate the best response. Consider hiring a nonprofit marketing agency to help you with this!

5. Don't Neglect Face-to-Face

So much of our day-to-day lives can be conducted online these days that we often forget the potency of in-person interaction. Getting out into the community may be more expensive than sending a quick email marketing campaign, but it has greater potential to strengthen your brand’s reputation and attract loyal donors, supporters and volunteers.

6. Be Fearless but aligned

People remember things that are out of the ordinary, so don't be afraid to innovate and do things a little differently.  It goes without saying that anything you consider doing should be consistent with your purpose, mission statement and brand strategy and be genuinely innovative rather than different - just for the sake of it. In fact nonprofits and startups are quite similar in this regard. They are both often powered by very passionate, dedicated and self-motivated people with a clear view of where they want to go and how they want to get there.  But without a clear sense of purpose, direction and how they can each play their part in delivering the mission, startups and nonprofits can easily become fraught, fragmented and inefficient.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_button button_url="/contact-us/" button_text="CONTACT US" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" hover_enabled="0" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}" button_bg_color="#000000" button_bg_enable_color="on" button_bg_color__hover_enabled="on|hover" sticky_enabled="0" button_bg_color__hover="#ff08cc" button_bg_enable_color__hover="on" button_border_color__hover="#ff08cc"] [/et_pb_button][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_color_top="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_4_font="||on||||||" header_4_font_size="17px" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_layout="dark" global_colors_info="{}"]

 How Grounded Can Help You Develop Your Nonprofit Branding Plan

When we start working with a new nonprofit that wants to reinvigorate its brand and marketing strategy, we encourage them to create a BPP. This framework was initially developed for-profit and  startups but we have found that it is similarly effective for larger brands, corporations and social enterprises as well. 

Get in touch if you would like help developing your own BPP. It starts with discovery, a few stakeholder interviews, a workshop and some iteration and can usually be completed in 3-4 weeks.

The BPP framework (Belief, Purpose & Pursuits) unites the ‘why’ of purpose with the ‘way’ of fundraising. From the top down and bottom up, it synchronises people, process and performance behind purpose, providing the basis for innovation and the narrative for internal and external engagement. Its goal is to give brands and organizations the focus, inspiration and flexibility to think, behave and govern more responsibly. 

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="#414854" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_direction="110deg" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height="318px" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" background_size="initial" background_position="top_left" background_repeat="repeat" custom_margin="0px||20px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.17.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

How it works:

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.16" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

THE BELIEF:

This is what you believe about the world. It is the single-minded profoundly human belief that anchors your ambition and provides the inspiration for your purpose.

It should be human.
It should be truthful.
It should be universal.

And it should set-up a tension by addressing the problem or barrier for your purpose to resolve.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.18.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

THE PURPOSE:

This is what your organization or brand exists to do. Purpose flows through every level of the business. It should express how you will affect culture, serve people or change the world for the better in some way.

It should be simple.
It should be focussed.
It should be transformational.

It should make you feel that your job is never done and that you will never quite achieve it and ideally, it should stick in your throat because it means that much to you.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.16" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_margin="21px|||||" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

THE PURSUITS:

These are the three key things that your brand or organization should be actively pursuing day in and day out. They should drive decision-making, inform strategy, focus resources and in turn, help shape your values and behaviours.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_3,1_3,1_3" make_equal="on" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.18.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Product:

This is what your resources and talents should be focused on: producing, selling, and developing.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.17.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Platform:

This is the main communications and engagement vehicle that you use to go to market. It should empower the end user, customer, client or donor to control the kind of experience they want.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.17.0" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="RGBA(255,255,255,0)" custom_padding="0px|0px|0px|0px|true|true" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Impact:

The difference that your brand or organization can make in the world if your product and platform can generate a sustainable revenue.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_4_font="||on||||||" header_4_font_size="17px" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_layout="dark" global_colors_info="{}"]

Here’s an example:

OceanCycle is a startup, B-corp certified, social enterprise.

BELIEF:

We will only put an end to ocean plastic by 2030 if we stop the flow of new plastic entering the ocean along with the marketing spin and misinformation around what can actually be retrieved and recycled.

PURPOSE:
‘Clean Up’ Ocean Plastic for Good.

PURSUITS:

Consultation:

We create marketplace demand for ocean bound plastic and work with clients to help them build the recycling infrastructures, ecosystems and supply chains necessary for driving scale and circularity.

Certification:

We certify ocean bound plastic material from collection through to manufacture — setting the standard, guaranteeing its authenticity and traceability across the entire chain of custody.

Community:

We help our clients realize the commercial value of ocean bound plastic through direct engagement with the communities where plastic is collected — resulting in jobs and income that improve livelihoods and lift families out of poverty.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/OC-logo.png" alt="ocean cycle company logo" title_text="OC-logo" align="center" _builder_version="4.23.1" _module_preset="default" width="85%" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/water-fork.jpg" alt="startup branding purpose - grounded world agency" title_text="startup branding purpose - grounded world agency" align="center" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" width="85%" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="#414854" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_direction="110deg" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height="318px" custom_padding="40px||40px||true|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_width_bottom="40px" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.16" background_size="initial" background_position="top_left" background_repeat="repeat" custom_margin="0px||20px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||30px||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.16" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

In summary

The BPP framework gives start-ups, brands, nonprofits and social enterprises the focus, inspiration and flexibility they need to turn branding into action. These actions can scale social innovations and campaigns into instruments that make the world a better place to be.

It is the first and most important step in the articulation of your core value proposition. And if done well, it can drive decision making from the inside-out. It connects what you believe about the world to your purpose. It transforms that purpose into profit through the pursuits and creates value by doing good. Branding for startups is really all about the integration of belief, purpose, identity, product, platform and impact.

Get in touch if you would like help developing your own BPP. It starts with discovery, a few stakeholder interviews, a workshop and some iteration and can usually be completed in 3-4 weeks.

Home 9 Search query for: 1 ( Page 10 )
A Complete Guide to Nonprofit Branding & Marketing
Learn more about nonprofit branding, marketing and the benefits of working with an agency for both. 
Contact us
Think about the WWF panda, Amnesty International’s flickering candle surrounded by barbed wire or the Peace Corps dove logo, which fuses the powerful imagery of the national flag and a globally recognised symbol of peace. Most would consider these good examples of the power of branding in the nonprofit sector.
Yet truly powerful branding in the deeper more cultural sense,  goes beyond a nonprofit’s visual identity. It’s more to do with the mission statement behind it all that underpins its role, meaning, difference and connection to the world and the people it serves. 
It’s that – as much as the visual identity – that people hold in their heads and why ultimately,  they put their hands in their pockets.
What is the role of branding and marketing for nonprofits and social enterprises?
How can a nonprofit marketing agencies and their branding efforts help to achieve wider goals? 

In an age where brand activism and cancel culture are growing,  along with the demand for products and services from companies whose values align more with our own, it is hardly surprising that branding and marketing for nonprofits and social enterprises is growing in complexity too. 
Previously,  branding and marketing for nonprofits often meant throwing your logo on a few pens and hoping that their repeated visibility and use for household shopping lists would mean that your organisation came to mind when people were feeling altruistic.  
In other words, branding was more of an executional tool for fundraising than anything else – rather than the reason the organization should exist or be fundraising in the first place. And today that’s the predicament many nonprofits still find themselves in. 
Good branding and marketing is often subservient to fundraising when in fact,  as any nonprofit marketing agency  will tell you,  to build equity and unlock revenue – the opposite should be the case. 
Today, an increasing number of nonprofits are exploring “the wider, strategic roles that brands can play: driving broad, long-term social goals, while strengthening internal identity, cohesion, and capacity.” It’s no longer about simply achieving fundraising targets through branding projects. 
Both established nonprofits and newly emerging ones are shifting the focus of their branding projects away from simply achieving fundraising targets and towards the achievement of social impact goals and greater equity, cultural and organisational cohesion. In essence, they are realising that having an MTP (massive transformative purpose) and uniting everyone in their organization to work towards this purpose,  is a far more valuable role for branding to play.
That’s good news for the industry, but even better news for people and the planet. 

How do you create a strong brand identity for a nonprofit or social enterprise?
In contrast to most for-profit brands, nonprofit organizations start with a desire to further their mission and make a sustainable impact, not make money. There are no shareholders, only stakeholders whose lives are often tied directly to the organization’s ability to serve them and raise money.
Think of World Food Programme, Unicef or St Jude’s Hospital or the Red Cross. Whether they are raising funds and awareness to feed children in wa-torn countries or funding research into cancer, they are established by people with a desire to make a positive difference in the world. But there are a lot of these good causes and a lot of organisations trying to advance very similar missions, 1.5m in the US alone. 
Creating a strong brand identity will enable you to differentiate yourself from your competition and talk to your target audience effectively to cultivate ongoing loyalty and trust. 
A nonprofit with a strong brand has better visibility, is more mentally accessible,  has a stronger reputation and so access to more resources and a greater influence on how those resources are deployed.  
Get the branding right and you will get more donations and have your pick of the smartest, most dedicated employees and volunteers.
Tips for Building a Strong Nonprofit Brand
1. Nail your brand purpose and mission statement (and yes, they are different)
Your purpose is why you exist and your mission statement is what you promise to do.  Both should be the heart of your nonprofit brand and you should refer back to them whenever you need to make important strategic decisions. Request input from donors, board members, stakeholders, employees and volunteers when crafting your brand purpose and mission statement, as this will facilitate valuable organizational cohesion. 
2. Know your Audience
Successful nonprofit branding comes from knowing your target audience. Take the time to understand, not only your current or potential donors, but also the people who may be prepared to give up their time to volunteer for your organization or spread the word about what you do. Tap into their beliefs, their mindsets and and their motivations for doing whatever they do and you will often find a universal belief or mindset that unites them all and provides a powerful platform for your purpose and future brand building.
3. Create a Nonprofit Branding Guide
When you have worked hard to establish exactly what your brand stands for and carefully designed and selected a logo, fonts, colours imagery and photography that you feel perfectly reflect your organization, the last thing you want is for someone to inadvertently send the wrong branding elements to press or use the wrong tone in an email campaign. Make it easy for employees and volunteers to get it right by creating a comprehensive branding guide, which lets them know what files to use where and provides clear guidance about the style and tone of each different content type your organization uses. 
4. Tell Your Stories 
Whilst your stats and the clarity of your communication will convince your audience that you are working hard and using their donated dollars to good effect, you also need to communicate the day-to-day challenges your nonprofit and its beneficiaries face and the best way to do that is through storytelling. 
These can be in social posts, videos, blog posts or printed material, just be honest (no one wants an exaggerated sales pitch) and remember that focusing on a main protagonist will always generate the best response. Consider hiring a nonprofit marketing agency to help you with this!
5. Don’t Neglect Face-to-Face
So much of our day-to-day lives can be conducted online these days that we often forget the potency of in-person interaction. Getting out into the community may be more expensive than sending a quick email marketing campaign, but it has greater potential to strengthen your brand’s reputation and attract loyal donors, supporters and volunteers. 
 
6. Be Fearless but aligned
People remember things that are out of the ordinary, so don’t be afraid to innovate and do things a little differently.  It goes without saying that anything you consider doing should be consistent with your purpose, mission statement and brand strategy and be genuinely innovative rather than different – just for the sake of it. In fact nonprofits and startups are quite similar in this regard. They are both often powered by very passionate, dedicated and self-motivated people with a clear view of where they want to go and how they want to get there.  But without a clear sense of purpose, direction and how they can each play their part in delivering the mission, startups and nonprofits can easily become fraught, fragmented and inefficient.
CONTACT US
 How Grounded Can Help You Develop Your Nonprofit Branding Plan
When we start working with a new nonprofit that wants to reinvigorate its brand and marketing strategy, we encourage them to create a BPP. This framework was initially developed for-profit and  startups but we have found that it is similarly effective for larger brands, corporations and social enterprises as well. 
Get in touch if you would like help developing your own BPP. It starts with discovery, a few stakeholder interviews, a workshop and some iteration and can usually be completed in 3-4 weeks.
The BPP framework (Belief, Purpose & Pursuits) unites the ‘why’ of purpose with the ‘way’ of fundraising. From the top down and bottom up, it synchronises people, process and performance behind purpose, providing the basis for innovation and the narrative for internal and external engagement. Its goal is to give brands and organizations the focus, inspiration and flexibility to think, behave and govern more responsibly. 
How it works:
THE BELIEF:
This is what you believe about the world. It is the single-minded profoundly human belief that anchors your ambition and provides the inspiration for your purpose.
It should be human.It should be truthful.It should be universal.
And it should set-up a tension by addressing the problem or barrier for your purpose to resolve.
THE PURPOSE:
This is what your organization or brand exists to do. Purpose flows through every level of the business. It should express how you will affect culture, serve people or change the world for the better in some way.
It should be simple.It should be focussed.It should be transformational.
It should make you feel that your job is never done and that you will never quite achieve it and ideally, it should stick in your throat because it means that much to you.
THE PURSUITS:
These are the three key things that your brand or organization should be actively pursuing day in and day out. They should drive decision-making, inform strategy, focus resources and in turn, help shape your values and behaviours.
The Product:
This is what your resources and talents should be focused on: producing, selling, and developing.
The Platform:
This is the main communications and engagement vehicle that you use to go to market. It should empower the end user, customer, client or donor to control the kind of experience they want.
The Impact:
The difference that your brand or organization can make in the world if your product and platform can generate a sustainable revenue.
Here’s an example:
OceanCycle is a startup, B-corp certified, social enterprise.
BELIEF:
We will only put an end to ocean plastic by 2030 if we stop the flow of new plastic entering the ocean along with the marketing spin and misinformation around what can actually be retrieved and recycled.
PURPOSE:‘Clean Up’ Ocean Plastic for Good.
PURSUITS:
Consultation:
We create marketplace demand for ocean bound plastic and work with clients to help them build the recycling infrastructures, ecosystems and supply chains necessary for driving scale and circularity.
Certification:
We certify ocean bound plastic material from collection through to manufacture — setting the standard, guaranteeing its authenticity and traceability across the entire chain of custody.
Community:
We help our clients realize the commercial value of ocean bound plastic through direct engagement with the communities where plastic is collected — resulting in jobs and income that improve livelihoods and lift families out of poverty.
In summary
The BPP framework gives start-ups, brands, nonprofits and social enterprises the focus, inspiration and flexibility they need to turn branding into action. These actions can scale social innovations and campaigns into instruments that make the world a better place to be.
It is the first and most important step in the articulation of your core value proposition. And if done well, it can drive decision making from the inside-out. It connects what you believe about the world to your purpose. It transforms that purpose into profit through the pursuits and creates value by doing good. Branding for startups is really all about the integration of belief, purpose, identity, product, platform and impact.
Get in touch if you would like help developing your own BPP. It starts with discovery, a few stakeholder interviews, a workshop and some iteration and can usually be completed in 3-4 weeks.
Stay Grounded with us!

Our Approach

Roll over the tiles to see how we can help you.

 

[/et_pb_text][simp_our_approach heading_1="Research" content_1="

Assessing culture and the competitive landscape to find the intention-action gaps.

  • Culture, category, competitor and consumer landscape assessments
  • Intent-to-action gap research and analysis
  • Ecosystem mapping, need states, category growth drivers & go to market strategy
" heading_2="Strategy" content_2="

Connecting the ‘why’ of purpose to the ‘way’ of profit to tell your sustainability story and drive innovation.

  • Corporate, brand and social purpose articulation
  • Sustainability strategy, marketing, corporate narrative, storytelling and innovation sprints
  • Brand positioning architecture & archetyping
" heading_3="Activation" content_3="

Driving behavior change and intent at the moments that matter for colleagues, customers and consumers.

  • Brand identity, packaging, websites & design
  • Content, video, advertising, campaigns, events and experiences
  • Commerce (customer journeys, selling stories, brand and retail activation and tactical toolkits)
" heading_4="Impact" content_4="

Measuring impact, reporting on progress sparking engagement and facilitating collaboration and partnerships at scale

  • Sustainability & impact reporting
  • Commercial, non-profit and pre-competitive partnerships
  • Collaboration training, action labs, design sprints, speaking engagements and thought leadership

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
We’re a Non Profit Marketing Agency
Do you have a social purpose, mission or manifesto that needs to be revitalized? Do you know how to activate it in a culturally relevant way to unlock unrestricted donations, galvanize key stakeholders and drive behavior change at scale? We can help you accelerate your impact.
Book A Call
Get Grounded
We are a multi award winning B Corp certified social innovation and brand activation agency – thriving at the intersection of brand purpose, commercial innovation and social impact. We work with brands, retailers, startups and nonprofits all over the world – helping them to articulate their purpose, activate their brands and accelerate their impact.
Our Approach
Roll over the tiles to see how we can help you.
 

Research

Assessing culture and the competitive landscape to find the intention-action gaps.

Culture, category, competitor and consumer landscape assessments
Intent-to-action gap research and analysis
Ecosystem mapping, need states, category growth drivers & go to market strategy

Find the Gap

Strategy

Connecting the ‘why’ of purpose to the ‘way’ of profit to tell your sustainability story and drive innovation.

Corporate, brand and social purpose articulation
Sustainability strategy, marketing, corporate narrative, storytelling and innovation sprints
Brand positioning architecture & archetyping

Become more Sustain-Agile

Activation

Driving behavior change and intent at the moments that matter for colleagues, customers and consumers.

Brand identity, packaging, websites & design
Content, video, advertising, campaigns, events and experiences
Commerce (customer journeys, selling stories, brand and retail activation and tactical toolkits)

Activate your brand

Impact

Measuring impact, reporting on progress sparking engagement and facilitating collaboration and partnerships at scale

Sustainability & impact reporting
Commercial, non-profit and pre-competitive partnerships
Collaboration training, action labs, design sprints, speaking engagements and thought leadership

Accelerate your Impact
  • Culture, category, competitor and consumer landscape assessments
  • Intent-to-action gap research and analysis
  • Good growth drivers & go to market strategy
Contact Us
  • Corporate, brand & social purpose
  • Sustainability strategy, marketing, narrative and storytelling
  • Brand positioning, architecture & archetyping
Contact Us
  • Brand identity, packaging & design
  • Campaigns, events, experiences and key stakeholder engagement
  • Retail activation (selling stories customer Journeys, omnichannel toolkits, loyalty marketing and tactical plans)
Contact Us
  • Sustainability & impact reporting
  • Commercial and non-profit partnerships
  • Collaboration training, action labs, design sprints, speaking engagements and thought leadership
Contact Us

Not quite right… need to do something else?

Click on the most relevant tag to reveal the roadmap. You can expand each of the four phases to see each step. You may not need all of them…but at least you can see the full picture.

Leave us your Company email address (so we know that you are real) and your roadmap will be revealed!

Although no substitute for expert legal advice or counsel, Gaia is our resident AI and she can answer any questions you might have about the white paper or the broader sustainability, policy and legislative challenges surrounding the fashion, textile and apparel industry. If you leave your company email address when consulting with her, she'll even develop a set of recommendations tailored to your specific brand or business.
