wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Policy to Profit:How New Rules Can Create Commercial Wins for Fashion
Thanks for dropping in! This resource center will help you navigate the new world of sustainability reporting, legislation, and disclosures to help you transform policy into profit. 
These downloads will equip you with the data, insight, tools, and inspiration to close the intention-action gap and commercialize sustainability for your business.
Here, you’ll find:
Clear, actionable insights and analysis of essential sustainability policies and legislation across governance, environmental, and social pillars
The pathways that brands, retailers, and manufacturers need to navigate this moment of transition surrounding the regulatory maze
Practical guidance to drive commercial innovation while maximizing your triple bottom line impact
Take me to:
Whitepaper
Infographic
Podcast
Ask Gaia
Whitepaper
Informed by expert legal counsel along with expert interviews, this whitepaper will equip you with the data, insight, guidelines, and practical steps to help you close the intention-action gap, navigate policy and legislation, and commercialize sustainability.
Download the Whitepaper
Infographic
This infographic lays out all the key components and considerations you need to start building a flywheel of triple bottom line impact, covering all the key aspects of E, S & G.
Download the Infographic
PODCAST
Meet our co-founders, Phil White & Heidi Schoeneck as they interview experts from across the industry and share tips and insight about how to navigate risk, take first steps or maintain a leadership position. 
INTRODUCING GAIA
Although no substitute for expert legal advice or counsel, Gaia is our resident AI and she can answer any questions you might have about the white paper or the broader sustainability, policy and legislative challenges surrounding the fashion, textile and apparel industry. If you leave your company email address when consulting with her, she’ll even develop a set of recommendations tailored to your specific brand or business.

A Social Impact Marketing Agency Guide To:

How to Drive Purpose and Profit with a Social Impact Marketing Agency

A step-by-step introduction to Social Impact Marketing: Turning your vision into meaningful action.

What is Social Impact Marketing?

At Grounded, we define social impact marketing as the positive impact specific acts or initiatives have on people, planet, and communities. For example, building a pump for clean water in a remote Kenyan community can improve hygiene and health. This can increase girls' school attendance and boost the community's prosperity!

What Does A Social Impact Marketing Agency Do?

A Social Impact Marketing Agency blends traditional marketing expertise with a deep commitment to driving meaningful change. These agencies specialize in helping brands elevate their Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) from a secondary concern to a core part of their identity and business strategy.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

What Sets Social Impact Marketing Agencies Apart?

  • Behavioral Insight: Leverage expertise in human behavior to create campaigns that resonate deeply with audiences.

  • Purpose-Driven Focus: Prioritize initiatives that align brand and business  strategy with social, environmental, or cultural transformation.

  • Holistic Support: Drawing on a diverse range of resources to help clients:
    — Motivate customers and stakeholders.
    — Build authentic engagement around shared values.
    — Create systematic change on a social, environmental, or cultural level.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_line_height="1.4em" background_layout="dark" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

Unlike traditional marketing agencies, Social Impact Marketing Agencies aim to build brands not just for profit, but to inspire action and drive transformation.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.27.4" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_line_height="1.3em" header_4_font="||||||||" header_4_font_size="17px" header_4_line_height="1.3em" background_color="#000000" use_background_color_gradient="on" background_color_gradient_stops="rgba(42,125,214,0) 49%|#0a0101 100%" background_color_gradient_overlays_image="on" background_image="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/plan-International.jpg" background_position="top_center" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="||0px||false|false" custom_padding="100px|20px|0px|20px|false|true" link_option_url="https://grounded.world/project/plan-international/" link_option_url_new_window="on" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

How Can a Business Create Social Impact?

There is an outdated but persistent belief that conscious companies committed to positive social impact do so at the expense of their profit margins. But, strong social impact marketing strategies need not limit your business goals or profits, and can help reinforce your brand identity and positioning. 

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

It is possible to make money and make a difference!

The world’s biggest problems – poverty, pollution, inequality, climate change, political polarization, or human rights – are also the biggest business opportunities. They provide the need and opportunity to demonstrate how companies can transform the WHY of purpose into the WAY of profit.  And, nowhere is this mindset (and skillset) better represented and captured as a philosophy (and an operating system) than an  Exponential Organization.

The Role of Exponential Organizations (ExOs) in Social Impact

What is an ExO?

An ExO (Exponential Organization) is a purpose-driven, agile, and scalable business model that achieves extraordinary results by leveraging cutting-edge technologies and innovative strategies. The ExO model empowers organizations to:

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="0px||||false|false" custom_padding="0px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]
  • Achieve Rapid Growth: Leverages exponential technologies and agile structures to outpace competitors.
  • Adapt to Change: Thrives in the face of 21st-century challenges, including global poly-crises.
  • Deliver Exceptional Results: Generates up to 40x the shareholder returns compared to traditional enterprises.
[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

A Culture of “Benevolent Disruption”

An ExO isn’t just about growth—it’s about transforming industries while creating a positive impact.

  • Tribes of Passionate Employees and Customers: United by a shared vision and mission.
  • Benevolent Disruptors: Challenging industry norms to make the world better.
[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.27.4" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/exponential.png" title_text="exponential" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" specialty="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font="|700|||||||" header_2_line_height="1.2em" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_4_line_height="1.2em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="0px||20px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"]

Step 2: Articulate Your Massive Transformative Purpose (MTP)

Achieving exponential social impact starts with articulating your MTP. Grounded’s open-source tool called the BPP (Belief, Purpose, and Pursuits) is published by the ANA as the best-in-class framework to enable brands, retailers, start-ups, and nonprofits worldwide to help them articulate their MTP.

Step 3: Connect To The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font="|700|||||||" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="||||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

What Are the SDGs?

The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a universal call to action established by the United Nations to address the world’s most pressing challenges by 2030. Comprised of 17 interconnected goals, the SDGs aim to:

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="0px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]
  • End poverty and hunger.
  • Improve health, education, and overall well-being.
  • Reduce inequalities and promote economic growth.
  • Combat climate change.
  • Protect oceans, forests, and biodiversity.
[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="0px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

Often referred to as the ‘business plan to save the world,’ the SDGs provide a clear roadmap for organizations to align their strategies with global priorities, balancing profit with positive societal and environmental impact.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_css_main_element="align-self: center;||" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/global-goals.png" title_text="global goals" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" position_origin_a="top_right" position_origin_f="bottom_right" width="100%" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" use_custom_gutter="on" specialty="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" custom_css_main_element="display: flex;||flex-wrap: wrap;" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font="|700|||||||" header_2_line_height="1.2em" header_3_text_color="#4dd9ff" header_4_line_height="1.2em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="0px||20px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"]

A Framework for Business and Impact

For companies navigating stricter policies and legislation around climate and supply chain disclosures, the SDGs offer a powerful framework for:

    • Sustainability Reporting: Aligning corporate activities with global goals to demonstrate social and environmental impact transparently.
    • Governance and Accountability: Helping boards, investors, and regulators see how commercial objectives align with fiduciary, environmental, and social responsibilities.
    • Strategic Alignment: Positioning businesses as leaders in sustainable growth while addressing the expectations of diverse stakeholders.

By leveraging the SDGs, organizations can effectively align their business goals with the broader mission of creating a fairer, healthier, and more sustainable world.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" specialty_columns="2" _builder_version="4.16" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_row_inner column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="10%|||0px|false|false" custom_padding="0px||0px|30px|false|false" custom_css_main_element="display: flex;||flex-wrap: wrap;||" border_style_left="dotted" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column_inner type="1_2" saved_specialty_column_type="1_2" admin_label="Button" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_css_main_element="align-self: center;||" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_font="|700|||||||" header_3_font="|700|||||||" header_3_text_color="#4dd9ff" background_layout="dark" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

I'm Often Asked...

[/et_pb_text][dipl_text_animator prefix_text=" " animated_text="What essential qualities and processes should you look for when choosing a social impact marketing agency" postfix_text=" " display_tag="h3" animation_layout="typing" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_text_settings_text_color="#FFFFFF" min_height="140px" link_option_url="https://grounded.world/gaia/?question=q6" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"] [/dipl_text_animator][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_5" _builder_version="4.27.4" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="10%||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/gaia-face-faded.jpg" title_text="gaia-face-faded" show_bottom_space="off" align_tablet="" align_phone="center" align_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" width="75%" width_tablet="75%" width_phone="70%" width_last_edited="on|phone" module_alignment="left" global_colors_info="{}"] [/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.27.5" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||40px|false|false" custom_padding_tablet="|||40px|false|false" custom_padding_phone="|||0px|false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" border_color_left="gcid-primary-color" border_style_left="dotted" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-primary-color%22:%91%22border_color_left%22%93}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_accordion closed_toggle_text_color="#FFFFFF" closed_toggle_background_color="#000000" icon_color="#ee2a7b" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="20px" _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" border_width_all="0px" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_accordion_item title="1. Do They Understand Your Brand’s Purpose?" open="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||"]

A good place to start is to make sure the team knows what your brand purpose isn’t.

  • It isn’t your brand essence, mission, proposition, or marketing slogan.
  • It doesn’t include words like “you”, “we” or “us” and won’t talk about the brand specifically.
  • Rather, brand purpose is “a higher-order reason for a brand’s existence that goes beyond making money and adds value to society.”

In short, ensure your social impact agency understands brand purpose and its role and application. Ideally, they will help you define it.

Discover our Belief, Purpose & Pursuits framework tool to help define your brand purpose.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="2. How Innovative is the Agency?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

The world’s biggest problems are also the world’s biggest business opportunities. Because of this, social impact marketing agencies are becoming experts in social innovation, as well as in social impact. After all, they are tasked with inventing and creating new products, platforms, and solutions to some of humanity’s greatest challenges.

Great examples of what’s possible:

  • MIT Solve
  • OPENIDEO
[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="3. Who Are Their Clients?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

Seek out an agency with social impact clients that include: 

  • For-profits
  • Non-profits
  • Startups
  • Venture capital groups 
  • Foundations

Ask the agency to provide examples of the ways in which they have facilitated and activated partnerships across sectors – because cross-sector collaboration drives innovation.

A good social impact agency will be willing to make introductions and connections among their clients to generate collaborative action and even greater impact.

    [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="4. Does the Agency Walk the Talk?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

    The agency’s team should be passionate about making a difference in the world and have a genuine commitment to impact society positively. Many social impact agencies are made up of activists and social enterprises who openly support social and environmental causes they care about.

    Look for a fearlessly creative agency that operates with transparency, is willing to educate and freely share knowledge, and has a network of like-minded partners. 

    At Grounded, we’re very proud of our Certified B Corp status. Certified B Corporations are a collective of purpose-led companies that balance principle and profit. They are legally required to consider the effect of their decisions on their workers, customers, suppliers, community, and the environment. This is a community of leaders, driving a global movement of people using business as a force for good.

      [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="5. What Are Their Digital and Social Capabilities?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

      Impact and activism are happening online these days, so working with an agency that is well-versed in all things digital, social, and influencer will be key to your success. 

      From cutting-edge digital content, omnichannel customer service, and water-tight security management services, your agency needs to be familiar with all the online innovations that will facilitate the delivery of your brand purpose.

        [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="6. Who Are Their Partners?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h4" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

        Research the individuals and organizations the agency is affiliated with and with whom they regularly collaborate. 

        They should have a list posted on their site and, if not, ask for it. Are they willing to leverage these relationships with you?  

        At Grounded, we are happy to call upon our connections at: 

        • The UN Office of Partnerships
        • Sustainable Brands 
        • Open ExO
        • ANA
        • Real Leaders
        • 1% for the Planet and 

        The B-Corp community, amongst others.

          [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="7. What’s Their Process?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

          At Grounded, our process with clients begins with a landscape assessment, articulating the problem and identifying the opportunities. 

          Once we align and have a game plan, we move on to purpose articulation which then drives internal and external activation, bringing a client’s story to life and measuring impact on relevant stakeholders. 

          Using quantitative and qualitative research, we’ll then map out the desired consumer experience to identify the moments that matter, build the campaign and tactical plan, plugging in partners, tech, and digital to accelerate impact and power transformation for your brand.

            [/et_pb_accordion_item][/et_pb_accordion][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.23.1" header_2_font_size="64px" header_2_line_height="78px" text_orientation="center" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="||0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="||0px||false|false" header_2_line_height_tablet="60px" header_2_line_height_phone="48px" header_2_line_height_last_edited="on|tablet" global_colors_info="{}"]

            About Grounded

            Grounded is a multi-award-winning boutique B Corp marketing agency with decades of experience in design, branding activation, sustainability marketing, and social impact. We have helped some of the world’s best-known brands, retailers, non-profits, and start-ups articulate their purpose, activate their brands, and accelerate their impact.

            Our team comprises seasoned professionals dedicated to continuous improvement and transformative ideas. We are certified ExO masters, enabling us to help businesses grow their impact 10x faster, better, and more cost-effectively than the competition.

            [/et_pb_text][et_pb_button button_url="@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF9saW5rX3VybF9wYWdlIiwic2V0dGluZ3MiOnsicG9zdF9pZCI6IjI0MSJ9fQ==@" url_new_window="on" button_text="Contact Us" button_alignment="center" _builder_version="4.27.4" _dynamic_attributes="button_url" button_bg_color="gcid-secondary-color" custom_margin="10px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-secondary-color%22:%91%22button_bg_color%22%93}" button_bg_enable_color__hover="on" button_text_size__hover_enabled="on|desktop"][/et_pb_button][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section]

hover_enabled="0" sticky_enabled="0"]

A Brand Purpose Agency Guide to:

Understanding and Activating Brand Purpose

Learn how a brand purpose agency can help you close the intention-action gap and accelerate impact by getting brands and retailers working more collaboratively to drive the right behavior change.

 

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" use_custom_gutter="on" gutter_width="3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" max_width="100%" module_alignment="center" custom_padding="5%||3%||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

Welcome to our guide to activating brand purpose at retail. This article and accompanying framework will help you develop campaigns and behavior-changing ideas that align with your brand purpose, resonate with your audience, and drive meaningful results by focusing on the interaction of three essential elements:

  • What your brand stands for:  Your brand's purpose, mission or transformational benefit (MISSION)
  • What the world needs: The social or environmental issue that matters most to your customers (or the broader community) that your brand purpose enbales you the credibly and uniquely solve? (MAGIC)
  • Your biggest intention-action gap: Find and overcome the biggest barrier preventing shoppers from selecting your brand and/or taking positive action that will enable you to drive purchase (MONEY)

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" text_font="|700|||||||" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="30px" text_line_height="1.3em" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font_size="30px" header_2_line_height="1.2em" background_color="#ff08cc" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="0px|-100px|0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px|10px|||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding="60px|60px|60px|60px|true|true" custom_padding_tablet="50px|50px|50px|50px|true|true" custom_padding_phone="50px|30px|50px|30px|true|true" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" hover_enabled="0" header_2_font_size_tablet="28px" header_2_font_size_phone="25px" header_2_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}" sticky_enabled="0" text_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" text_font_size_phone="26px" text_font_size_tablet="30px"]

“Retailers, as the main link between consumers and their products, are imperative to communicating sustainable products and increasing their demand.”

Marketing Sustainable Products in the Retail Sector

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" specialty="on" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#262626" background_enable_image="off" width_tablet="" width_phone="" width_last_edited="on|phone" custom_margin="||||false|false" custom_padding="30px||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="30px||0px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="35px||0px||false|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/social-impact-graphic-1trans.png" title_text="social-impact-graphic-1trans" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="left" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" specialty_columns="2" _builder_version="4.16" custom_padding="|||" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_row_inner column_structure="1_2,1_2" use_custom_gutter="on" gutter_width="1" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column_inner type="1_2" saved_specialty_column_type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="30px||30px||true|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

The Flywheel of Impact

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column_inner][et_pb_column_inner type="1_2" saved_specialty_column_type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/fly.png" title_text="fly" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" width="50%" custom_margin="|||10px|false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column_inner][/et_pb_row_inner][et_pb_row_inner column_structure="undefined" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" border_color_bottom="gcid-primary-color" border_style_bottom="dotted" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-primary-color%22:%91%22border_color_bottom%22%93}"][et_pb_column_inner type="undefined" saved_specialty_column_type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="||||true|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

The intersection of these three key elements (MISSION, MONEY AND MAGIC) can in turn deliver a triple bottom line win for people, planet, and profit.

We call it the Flywheel of impact because it’s capable of scaling both revenue and impact - enabling you to meaningfully activate your brand purpose and ultimately transform that brand purpose into profit. 

This is what a good brand purpose agency should be able to help you do - by combining insight, strategy, commercial innovation and tactical communications planning to activate your brand purpose and accelerate your impact - commercially, socially and environmentally.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column_inner][/et_pb_row_inner][et_pb_row_inner column_structure="1_2,1_2" use_custom_gutter="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" custom_css_main_element="display: flex;||flex-wrap: wrap;||" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column_inner type="1_2" saved_specialty_column_type="1_2" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="0px||0px||true|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

Download this handy one page guide to find out more

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column_inner][et_pb_column_inner type="1_2" saved_specialty_column_type="1_2" admin_label="Button" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" 0%|rgba(0,0,0,0.52) 100%" background_color_gradient_start="rgba(0,0,0,0.23)" background_color_gradient_end="rgba(0,0,0,0.52)" min_height_tablet="" min_height_phone="" min_height_last_edited="on|tablet" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="20px||40px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="20px||40px||false|false" z_index_tablet="500" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" box_shadow_horizontal_tablet="0px" box_shadow_vertical_tablet="0px" box_shadow_blur_tablet="40px" box_shadow_spread_tablet="0px" locked="off" admin_label="Text" _builder_version="4.27.4" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="10%||3%||false|false" custom_css_main_element="align-self: center;" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_3_text_color="#4dd9ff" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="||20px||false|false" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

Understanding Brand Purpose: What Does My Brand Stand For?

What is Brand Purpose?

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="0px||||false|false" custom_padding="0px||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"]

A simple definition of brand purpose is:

“A higher-order reason for a brand's existence that goes beyond making money and adds value to society.”

Digging a little deeper, brand purpose is:

  • The brand's reason for being – what does your brand ultimately exist to do?
  • A social impact or ambition that's bigger than the brand itself – How does your brand create a better world that benefits humankind in some way?
  • A motive beyond simply making money – A recent Edelman Trust Barometer found that 71% of respondents said they'd lose trust in a brand if it put its profits over people!
[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/about-us-focus-1.jpg" title_text="about-us-focus" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#262626" background_enable_image="off" width_tablet="" width_phone="" width_last_edited="on|phone" custom_margin="||||false|false" custom_padding="0px||0%||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="" custom_padding_phone="35px||0px||false|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" use_custom_gutter="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" max_width="100%" module_alignment="center" custom_padding="0px||2%||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_3_font="|700|||||||" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="25%||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

Why Brand Purpose Matters

The eXo (Exponential Organization) community refers to Brand Purpose as an MTP or (Massive Transformative Purpose). If articulated well and embedded into the DNA of the brand or organization, the MTP scales impact, reduces costs, and gives purpose-driven companies a competitive advantage - delivering finanical benefits for all stakeholders.

“Massive Transformative Purpose (MTP) describes a better future for the world (or at least your industry or community). It doesn't specify how. It's not about you, your customers, your organization, your products or services. No ‘you', ‘we' or ‘us'. You are not in the picture. It is not a marketing slogan. Consider it your north star, but one that doesn't restrict your organization from changing direction. It might excite and scare you, and catch in your throat, it matters that much to you. You might never fully achieve it, yet it is still worth striving for. Generally speaking, a great MTP attracts the customers, community, partners, and resources you need to make a dent in the universe…”

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/exponential.png" title_text="exponential" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#000000" background_enable_image="off" width_tablet="" width_phone="" width_last_edited="on|phone" custom_margin="||||false|false" custom_padding="0px||0%||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="" custom_padding_phone="35px||0px||false|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="4_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="15.5px" text_line_height="1.5em" header_3_text_color="#4dd9ff" background_layout="dark" custom_margin_tablet="" custom_margin_phone="" custom_margin_last_edited="on|tablet" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

Brand Purpose Examples from Leading Companies

Companies seeking to create a strong brand purpose should look to some of the world's biggest companies who have managed to articulate a compelling brand purpose effectively:

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_4,1_4,1_4,1_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/apple-180-1.jpg" title_text="apple-180" show_bottom_space="off" align="center" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" width="40%" custom_padding="0px||10px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_blurb title="Steve Jobs articulated Apple's purpose like this…" image_icon_width="36%" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" header_line_height="1.2em" text_orientation="center" background_layout="dark" global_colors_info="{}"]

“At Apple, we create tools for the mind that advance humankind.”

[/et_pb_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_4" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/lego-logo-black-and-white.png" title_text="lego-logo-black-and-white" show_bottom_space="off" align="center" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" width="40%" custom_padding="0px||10px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_blurb title="Similarly, Lego's is…" image_icon_width="50%" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" header_line_height="1.2em" text_orientation="center" background_layout="dark" global_colors_info="{}"]

“Inspiring the builders of tomorrow.”

What is Brand Activation?

Activating brand purpose requires far more focus and singularity than simply articulating it. Without access to both the "real estate" and consumer attention to tell your story, your brand purpose may not resonate—especially in a fast moving retail context.

The Challenge of Consumer Attention

The biggest challenge is the shopper’s demand for convenience and short attention span while shopping, whether in physical stores or online. Brands have only a brief opportunity to:

  • Grab the shopper’s attention.
  • Communicate their brand purpose and values effectively.
  • Communicate the right message, offer, or incentive to change behavior

Without addressing this challenge, even the best intentions may fail to register with habit-driven or task-focused shoppers.

Studying Shopping Behavior

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

To succeed, brands must take the time to understand how their shoppers behave along the entire path to purchase. 

This involves:

  • Identifying the biggest intention-action gap, or what prevents shoppers from making sustainable purchasing decisions.
  • Exploring the tension between what shoppers truly want and what stops them from making better choices.
  • Using these insights to develop the right message or role for the brand to play at retail.
[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_4" admin_label="Text" _builder_version="4.27.4" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||20px|false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" border_color_right="gcid-primary-color" border_style_right="dotted" border_color_left="gcid-primary-color" border_style_left="dotted" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-primary-color%22:%91%22border_color_left%22,%22border_color_right%22%93}" custom_padding__hover="|||"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_3_line_height="1.2em" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="||1px||false|false" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

Better Buying Decisions

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

The best buying decision often emerges at the intersection of those three critical factors we mentioned above:

  • What your brand stands for
  • What the world needs
  • The biggest intention action gap

These intersections provide opportunities to align brand purpose with meaningful behavior change at retail, because no matter how small they seem, at scale they are high stakes and can have a massive impact

How to Address Consumer Barriers Through Purpose-Driven Brand Activation

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="1_2,1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="1_2" _builder_version="4.16" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

Closing the Intenion-Action Gap and Overcoming Barriers to Purchase

For many conscious consumers, the second most common challenge is not knowing where to start when forming sustainable buying habits. Brands and retailers can play a vital role in guiding consumers by co-creating and collaborating around the following principles 

Identifying Shared Sustainable Behaviors

A great starting point is to pinpoint a sustainable behavior that both the brand and retailer can support together. This shared focus:

  • Opens a dialogue to agree on strategic alignment.
  • Helps uncover any shortcomings in the existing brand, customer or retail experience.

By working together, brand and retail leaders can identify white space opportunities to build stronger customer relationships and enhance their sustainability efforts when thwir sustainability goals and commitments overlap or are intersectional

Bridging the Intention-Action Gap

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

The ultimate goal is to shift consumer behavior toward sustainable choices by addressing the barriers to action. Key challenges may include:

  • Low relevance: Shoppers may not see the product as relevant to their needs.
  • Disbelief: A lack of trust in claims about sustainability.
  • Emotional disconnect: Insufficient resonance with the consumer.

At a channel level, obstacles might include:

  • Inconvenience: Difficulty accessing or using the product.

Complexity: Confusion about category options and benefits.

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_4" admin_label="Text" _builder_version="4.27.5" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||20px|false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" hover_enabled="0" border_color_right="gcid-primary-color" border_style_right="dotted" border_color_left="gcid-primary-color" border_style_left="dotted" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-primary-color%22:%91%22border_color_left%22,%22border_color_right%22%93}" custom_padding__hover="|||" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding_phone="|||0px|false|false" sticky_enabled="0" custom_padding_tablet="|||20px|false|false"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_2_font="|700|||||||" header_2_line_height="1.2em" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_3_line_height="1.2em" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="||1px||false|false" custom_padding="||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" z_index_tablet="500" text_text_align="left" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Deploying Resources Effectively

      [/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" hover_enabled="0" global_colors_info="{}" sticky_enabled="0"]

      Traditionally, the intention-action gap is described as the shift from “awareness to consideration” in the consumer journey. But bridging this gap involves:

      • Considering the entire path to purchase from awareness to consumption and even recommendation and referral.
      • Focusing on the key moments that matter most to.shoppers along that path to purchase
      • Investing in  product development, packaging, display, or improving navigational education and inspiration in the category to ensure that shoppers are more likely to choose your brand

      By addressing these gaps, brands can ensure that shoppers' good intentions lead to action, fostering both category growth and sustainable impact.

      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_4" _builder_version="4.27.5" background_color="rgba(0,0,0,0)" custom_padding="|||20px|false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" hover_enabled="0" border_color_left="gcid-primary-color" border_style_left="dotted" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-primary-color%22:%91%22border_color_left%22%93}" custom_padding__hover="|||" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding_phone="|||0px|false|false" sticky_enabled="0" custom_padding_tablet="|||20px|false|false"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Intention.png" title_text="Intention" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" width="65%" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_3_text_color="#4dd9ff" header_3_line_height="1.2em" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="||||false|false" custom_padding="||||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Have a Question?

      Ask Gaia, our resident ai brand purpose expert by clicking on the question below.

animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Connecting the Category to the Need State

      Connecting your brand to the consumer’s ‘need state’ involves understanding what consumers truly want and finding ways to meet those needs while positively impacting society and the environment. This connection is essential for aligning sustainability goals with tangible outcomes.

      Delivering on Consumer Needs Through Sustainability

      Ask yourself: “How can our sustainable behavior help drive consumer needs and unlock category growth opportunities for our brand?”

      For example, Tide’s #TurnToCold campaign linked its sustainability goal—reducing energy use—with consumer priorities like superior cleaning performance and value. They achieved this by:

      • Highlighting $150 annual energy savings on retail displays.
      • Partnering with the NFL to showcase effective cold-water cleaning.
      • Promoting online guides for families with energy-saving tips.
      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="2_5" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/tide.png" title_text="tide" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/advert.jpg" title_text="advert" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" background_color="#262626" background_enable_image="off" width_tablet="" width_phone="" width_last_edited="on|phone" custom_margin="||||false|false" custom_padding="0px||0%||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="" custom_padding_phone="35px||0px||false|false" border_width_top="40px" border_color_top="#000000" border_color_bottom="#000000" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_row column_structure="2_3,1_3" use_custom_gutter="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" max_width="100%" module_alignment="center" custom_padding="0px||2%||false|false" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="2_3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_3_font="|700|||||||" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20%||0px||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" global_colors_info="{}"]

      Creating a Shared Plan of Action: Retail ASctivation for Good

      Bringing a brand to market in the retail space is a critical moment for action. Success hinges on ensuring everyone involved understands their roles and responsibilities. However, there can be differing expectations:

      [/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_3_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_4_font="|700|||||||" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="20px||||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]
      • Some retailers expect brands to take the lead by recommending ways to encourage responsible shopping and consumption and will charge them to ‘pay to play’ in the category through feature and display
      • Conversely, some brands rely on retailers to set clear expectations and will develop their activation ideas, selling strategies and tactics to help the retailer meet their own sustainability goals and commitments. This is a much better strategy because interests are better aligned and retailers will often offer more support by way of media and placement. 
      [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type="1_3" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_image src="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/image-video-graphic.jpg" title_text="image-video-graphic" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_image][et_pb_divider divider_style="dotted" divider_weight="6px" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][/et_pb_divider][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.5" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="18px" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_3_line_height="1.2em" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="20px||||false|false" custom_padding="||||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||30px||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" hover_enabled="0" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}" sticky_enabled="0"]

      Download this handy one page guide to find out more about Retail Activation For Good Desing Sprints

      [/et_pb_text][et_pb_button button_url="https://grounded.world/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/One-Pager_RAFG.pdf" url_new_window="on" button_text="Download" _builder_version="4.27.5" Sustainability Marketing Agency Guide To:

How-To Get Started with Sustainability Marketing

The Grounded guide on connecting your business with purpose with profit, driving consumer engagement, and supporting good growth.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure="3_5,2_5" use_custom_gutter="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" max_width="100%" module_alignment="center" custom_padding="5%||3%||false|false" custom_css_main_element="display: flex;||flex-wrap: wrap;" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_column type="3_5" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_css_main_element="align-self: center;||" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font="|700|||||||" header_2_text_color="#4dd9ff" header_2_line_height="1.2em" header_3_font="|700|||||||" header_3_text_color="#00aeef" header_3_line_height="1.2em" header_5_text_color="#000000" header_5_line_height="1.7em" background_layout="dark" custom_padding="||||false|false" custom_padding_tablet="||30px||false|false" custom_padding_phone="30px||||false|false" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="bottom" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

What is the Goal of Sustainability Marketing

Sustainability marketing isn’t only about encouraging people to offset their flights, use less plastic packaging, recycle more, or eat less meat…although these are all important!

Sustainability marketing involves promoting products and services that can have a verifiable, net positive impact on people and planet. This is usually done through claims, storytelling and campaigns. The goal of sustainability marketing is to create a ‘flywheel of impact that allows:

  • More people buy your sustainable and socially responsible products
  • Which in turn unlocks more profits for the company.
  • The company reinvests these profits back into more sustainable innovation – keeping the flywheel spinning
[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" text_font="|700|||||||" text_text_color="#FFFFFF" text_font_size="22px" text_line_height="1.2em" header_2_text_color="#FFFFFF" header_2_font_size="30px" header_2_line_height="1.2em" background_color="#ff08cc" background_layout="dark" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_margin_tablet="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_phone="0px||||false|false" custom_margin_last_edited="on|phone" custom_padding="40px|40px|40px|40px|true|true" custom_padding_tablet="50px|50px|50px|50px|true|true" custom_padding_phone="50px|30px|50px|30px|true|true" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" header_2_font_size_tablet="28px" header_2_font_size_phone="25px" header_2_font_size_last_edited="on|phone" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"]

“An organization should meet the needs of its present consumers without compromising the ability of future generations to fulfill their own needs”

Philip Kotler

Have a Question?

Ask Gaia, our resident ai sustainability marketing expert by clicking on the question below

Step 1 - Fly Wheel or Impact

Discover how to start by building your own flywheel of Impact and articulate your Sustainable Value Proposition (SVP)

Is There Demand for Sustainable Products?

The Sustainable Market Share Index is published every year by NYU Stern's Center for Sustainable Business, in partnership with IRI. 

The 2023 report reveals that:

  • Sustainable products now have an 18.5% market share, up 1.2 points from last year, despite high inflation and strong store brands. 
  • Sustainable products' market share has risen 4.8 points since 2015.
  • Products marketed as sustainable achieved a 5-YR CAGR of 9.9% vs. 6.4% for their conventional counterparts.
  • Sustainability-marketed products delivered approximately one-third of all CPG growth, despite representing less than one-fifth of the market share.

Who Cares About Sustainability? 

Do consumers care about sustainability? The short answer is - yes, but the ‘when’ and ‘what’ varies by category or industry, and the type of shopping trip people are on. 

  • If it's an emergency fill-up trip or a special occasion, immediate (or perceived) needs will drive the purchase. 
  • But, for a planned or routine trip, it's easier to influence people to switch to a more sustainable option.

And it’s not just climate activists, Gen Z or pioneers for social change who are invested in sustainability. A growing number of people across the board are prioritizing environmental responsibility in their consumer choices. 

Sustainability in purchasing matters to Gen Z (75%), Gen X (80%), and Boomers (78%). Terms like "organic" and "sustainably sourced" are trending, especially in fashion, and consumers are increasingly checking if companies have ethical practices.

What Are Some Of The Challenges Associated With Sustainability And Sustainability Marketing?

Business Management

Many brands and businesses struggle to 'commercialize sustainability.' Their sustainability goals don't make it from the board room or ESG report into the brand or business plan because the business case for sustainability can be hard to prove!

A good sustainability marketing agency will help a brand connect its sustainability goals to consumers' needs and purchase intent They can connect the dots through research, strategy, and creativity. Their sustainability marketing strategy should be able to clearly demonstrate how the WHY of purpose can become the WAY of profit.

Once done..many of these barriers and challenges get removed!

What Are Some Of The Challenges Associated With Sustainability And Sustainability Marketing?

Greenwashing

Greenwashing presents the biggest challenge for sustainability marketing. To avoid it brands must ensure that their sustainability claims can be verified by a third party and have a measurable impact on people or planet.

If, for example, you claim that your renewable bamboo toilet paper reduces carbon emissions but ignore accounting for your CEO's private jet flights, you risk damaging your reputation and being accused of greenwashing.

Here are 5 ways to avoid greenwashing (according to UN experts).

What Are Some Of The Challenges Associated With Sustainability And Sustainability Marketing?

Transparency

Sustainability marketing also requires a wider commitment to transparency. Your claims, operations and supply chain must be authentic, transparent, accountable, and traceable. Let your sustainability marketing agency ask the tough questions and find the skeletons in the closet. They can unpack the less-than-perfect parts of your operation and advise on what should or should not be communicated. Remember, without a good grasp of the intention-action gaps and key issues, there's always a risk of greenwashing!

Quality & Consistency

Another challenge to making your product more sustainable is that you cannot do so by removing features, downsizing the product, or compromising quality. Consumers want a sustainable product, but it must work as well as any unsustainable alternative. Buying sustainably cannot feel like a trade-off. Think of sustainability marketing as a 'gift with purchase.' It's a reason to feel good about buying a brand or product, that should enhance the product's utility, enjoyment, or brand experience.  Allbirds for example manage to blend style and aspiration with a total commitment to sustainability across their line of footwear.

Closing the Intention-Action Gap

What is the Intention-Action Gap?

What we say we'll do and what we do often differ. This is especially true when price, convenience, habit, or peer pressure come into play.

We call this the intention-action gap and it’s a core behavioral insight in sustainability marketing. At Grounded, we spend a lot of time finding, understanding and measuring it for clients. This can exist within the client's organization... internally, structurally, systemically, or culturally. It almost always exists at a certain stage of the consumer experience journey (CEX).

The adjacent chart from the Sustainable Marketing 2030 Report shows that 97% of us want to live more sustainably. But, only 13% of us take action to do so. And, it may not surprise you that the intention-action gap also varies by category!

Closing the Intention-Action Gap

How Do You Close the Intention-Action Gap?

  • First, determine the size of the intention-action gap (vs. your competition). 
  • Then, develop the right content, messaging, and tactics to close it. This will drive purchase intent in your customers. 
  • How? Traditional ATL and BTL marketing, digital marketing, SEO, influencer, and social media marketing, email marketing, events, and brand experiences can all help close the gap.

The world’s biggest sustainability marketing challenges are also the world’s biggest business opportunities! Working with a sustainability marketing agency partner will help you:

    • Communicate your eco-credentials to your customers and the wider world.
    • Establish yourself as a front-runner in sustainable business practices, and
    • Develop a sustainability strategy and work towards building a more regenerative business. 

Done well, this can  supercharge impact and profitability for your company.

  • Find a top sustainability marketing agency with strong industry connections. For all its lofty goals, it is a monetized industry too! 
  • Find out which organizations they belong to and get a list of the brands with which they have achieved success. Are they prepared to leverage these connections for the benefit of your business? For example, Grounded actively participates, or works closely, with:

global_colors_info="{}"]

How to Choose the Best Sustainability Marketing Agency

  • Make sure they get you and your business. They may have an impressive track record of award-winning campaigns. They may have success with some of the biggest brands and sustainable clients. But, if their grasp of your brand's ethos and goals isn't strong, keep looking for a sustainability marketing agency that does.
  • Look for a company that leads by example. If they tell brands how to behave but lack the accreditations and transparency themselves, then don't work with them! Engage an agency that is as committed to sustainability as you are. It takes one to trust one.
  • Don’t forget digital! Look for an agency that specializes in digital, social, and influencer marketing services. They should be able to use their digital marketing skills to help you by creating plans that include (but aren’t limited to) digital marketing, search engine optimization, influencer marketing, social media, and email marketing, amongst others.
  • The Best Sustainability Marketing  partner should be able to run Design sprints. Unlocking sustainability's commercial potential often requires fast, agile and innovative work. This means discovering, ideating, and creating through design sprints. They must quickly find scalable, viable solutions without the need for big teams or high retainers.

closed_toggle_text_color="#FFFFFF" closed_toggle_background_color="#000000" icon_color="#ee2a7b" use_icon_font_size="on" icon_font_size="20px" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" custom_margin="0px||0px||false|false" custom_padding="0px||0px||false|false" animation_style="slide" animation_direction="right" border_width_all="0px" border_color_all="#ff08cc" locked="off" global_colors_info="{}"][et_pb_accordion_item title="What is sustainability marketing?" open="on" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" custom_padding="||||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||"]

Sustainability marketing integrates environmental and social responsibility into core marketing practices, aiming to create positive impact while delivering customer value marketing. This approach aligns with sustainable marketing principles. It focuses on profit, but also on creating positive change for people and the planet. Unlike traditional marketing, which seeks quick sales, sustainability marketing values long-term benefits. It focuses on caring for the environment and engaging ethically with consumers. It’s directly related to green marketing, which focuses on eco-friendly practices and products.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="What is the difference between sustainability and CSR?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

Sustainability and Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) both aim for social and environmental responsibility. But, they approach it differently. CSR is a broader framework within a business. It covers ethical practices, employee welfare, and community engagement across departments.

Sustainability marketing, in contrast, embeds these practices into the marketing strategy. It fosters a consumer-oriented approach.

    [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="How can a sustainable marketing strategy make a positive impact?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

    Sustainable marketing strategies focus on promoting products and services with environmental and social benefits. Companies that practice sustainable marketing mix eco-friendly materials, sustainable living practices, and creative campaigns.

      [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="What is a sustainability marketing agency, and how can it benefit my business?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

      A sustainability marketing firm creates strategies to show environmental impact, sustainability efforts, and social contributions for sustainable brands. Partnering with a sustainable marketing agency can help businesses develop their brand values and effectively communicate their sustainability initiatives to build trust, drive engagement, and increase brand loyalty.

        [/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title="What is the difference between a sustainability marketing agency and a sustainable marketing agency?" _builder_version="4.27.4" _module_preset="default" body_text_color="#000000" body_font_size="15px" body_line_height="1.2em" custom_margin="||3px||false|false" global_colors_info="{}" toggle_level="h5" toggle_font="|700|||||||" open="off"]

        A sustainability marketing agency focuses on helping businesses communicate their commitment to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles through effective marketing strategies. These agencies typically work with organizations that aim to promote sustainable practices, products, or initiatives to a wider audience.

        On the other hand, sustainable marketing agencies prioritize adopting environmentally and ethically responsible practices within their own operations. While both types of agencies support sustainability goals, a sustainable agency ensures its internal processes—such as reducing carbon footprints, using eco-friendly materials, and maintaining ethical supply chains—align with sustainable principles.

        In short, sustainability marketing agencies help others showcase their sustainability efforts, while sustainable agencies focus on practicing what they preach within their own operations. Many successful sustainable agencies, such as Grounded, combine both approaches, making them leaders in ethical marketing practices.

          [/et_pb_accordion_item][/et_pb_accordion][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built="1" custom_padding_last_edited="on|phone" _builder_version="4.23.1"           About Grounded

          Grounded is a multi-award-winning boutique B Corp marketing agency with decades of experience in design, branding activation, sustainability marketing, and social impact. We have helped some of the world’s best-known brands, retailers, non-profits, and start-ups articulate their purpose, activate their brands, and accelerate their impact.

          Our team comprises seasoned professionals dedicated to continuous improvement and transformative ideas. We are certified ExO masters, enabling us to help businesses grow their impact 10x faster, better, and more cost-effectively than the competition.

          [/et_pb_text][et_pb_button button_url="@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF9saW5rX3VybF9wYWdlIiwic2V0dGluZ3MiOnsicG9zdF9pZCI6IjI0MSJ9fQ==@" url_new_window="on" button_text="Contact Us" button_alignment="center" _builder_version="4.27.4" _dynamic_attributes="button_url" button_bg_color="gcid-secondary-color" custom_margin="10px||0px||false|false" global_colors_info="{%22gcid-secondary-color%22:%91%22button_bg_color%22%93}" button_bg_enable_color__hover="on" button_text_size__hover_enabled="on|desktop" button_bg_color__hover_enabled="on|hover" button_bg_color__hover="#ff08cc" button_border_color__hover="#ff08cc"] [/et_pb_button][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section]
